title.raw: 卷七 孝桓帝紀 Volume 7: Annals of Emperor Xiaohuan

sentences.zh: 孝桓皇帝諱志，肅宗曾孫也。

translations.literal: The Filial and Mighty Emperor's taboo name was Zhi; he was a great-grandson of Suzong (Emperor Zhang).
translations.idiomatic: The emperor later canonized as Xiaohuan bore the personal name Zhi and was a great-grandson of Emperor Zhang.

sentences.zh: 祖父河間孝王開，父蠡吾侯翼，母匽氏。

translations.literal: His grandfather was Liu Kai, Prince Xiao of Hejian; his father was Liu Yi, Marquis of Liyi; his mother was of the Yan clan.
translations.idiomatic: His grandfather was Liu Kai, posthumously titled Prince Xiao of Hejian; his father was Liu Yi, Marquis of Liyi; his mother was a Lady of the Yan clan.

sentences.zh: 翼卒，帝襲爵為侯。

translations.literal: When Yi died, the emperor inherited the noble rank and became marquis.
translations.idiomatic: When Liu Yi died, he succeeded to the marquisate.

sentences.zh: 本初元年，梁太后徵帝到夏門亭，將妻以女弟。

translations.literal: In the first year of Benchu, Empress Dowager Liang summoned the emperor to Xiamen Pavilion, intending to take him as husband for her younger sister.
translations.idiomatic: In the first year of Benchu (146), Empress Dowager Liang summoned him to Xiamen Pavilion, intending to marry him to her younger sister.

sentences.zh: 會質帝崩，太后遂與兄大將軍冀定策禁中，閏月庚寅，使冀持節，以王青蓋車迎帝入南宮，其日即皇帝位，時年十五。

translations.literal: It happened that Emperor Zhi died; the empress dowager then, with her elder brother Grand General Liang Ji, fixed the succession within the forbidden palace; on gengyin in the intercalary month she sent Liang Ji bearing the staff of authority, and with a prince's green-canopied carriage welcomed the emperor into the Southern Palace; that day he assumed the imperial throne; he was fifteen sui old.
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor Zhi died, the empress dowager and her brother Grand General Liang Ji chose the next ruler inside the palace. On gengyin in the intercalary month she had Liang Ji, credential staff in hand, receive him in a royal carriage with the green canopy and escort him into the Southern Palace; he mounted the throne that same day, at the age of fifteen.

sentences.zh: 太后猶臨朝政。

translations.literal: The empress dowager still presided over court government.
translations.idiomatic: The empress dowager continued to hold the reins of government.

sentences.zh: 辛巳，謁高廟、光武廟。

translations.literal: On xinsi he visited the shrine of Gaozu and the shrine of Emperor Guangwu.
translations.idiomatic: On the day xinsi he paid his respects at the shrines of Han Gaozu and Emperor Guangwu.

sentences.zh: 丙戌，詔曰：『孝廉、廉吏皆當典城牧民，禁奸舉善，興化之本，恒必由之。

translations.literal: On bingxu an edict said: "Filial and Incorrupt candidates and incorrupt officials all ought to administer cities and shepherd the people, prohibit wickedness and promote the good; the root of raising transformation must constantly pass through this."
translations.idiomatic: On bingxu he issued an edict: "Men recommended as Filial and Incorrupt and officials noted for integrity are meant to govern districts and care for the people, curb wrongdoing and encourage virtue; civilizing the realm always begins with them."

sentences.zh: 詔書連下，分明懇惻，而在所翫習，遂至怠慢，選舉乖錯，害及元元。

translations.literal: Edicts have been issued in succession, clear and earnest, yet where they are applied people have grown accustomed and negligent, so that selection and recommendation go awry and harm reaches the common people.
translations.idiomatic: Edict after edict has spelled this out in plain, urgent terms, yet local offices treat it as routine and grow slack; recommendations go wrong, and ordinary folk pay the price.

sentences.zh: 頃雖頗繩正，猶未懲改。

translations.literal: Recently although there has been some correction by the cord, still there has not been warning and reform.
translations.idiomatic: Some abuses have lately been checked, but the lesson has not sunk in.

sentences.zh: 方今淮夷未殄，軍師屢出，百姓疲悴，困於徵發。

translations.literal: Now the Huai tribes are not yet exterminated; armies go out repeatedly; the hundred surnames are weary and emaciated, distressed by levies and conscription.
translations.idiomatic: The Huai region is still unsettled, campaigns follow one another, and the people are worn down by taxes and drafts.

sentences.zh: 庶望群吏，惠我勞民，蠲滌貪穢，以祈休祥。

translations.literal: It is hoped that the host of officials will show kindness to our toiling people, wash away greed and filth, in order to pray for auspicious blessing.
translations.idiomatic: I look to every official to show mercy to a burdened populace, root out corruption, and so help bring peace and good fortune.

sentences.zh: 其令秩滿百石，十歲以上，有殊才異行，乃得參選。

translations.literal: Let it be ordered that only those whose rank is full one hundred shi and who have ten years or more, possessing outstanding talent and unusual conduct, may then participate in selection.
translations.idiomatic: Henceforth only those who have held rank of at least one hundred shi for ten years or more, and who show exceptional ability or conduct, may stand for recommendation.

sentences.zh: 臧吏子孫，不得察舉。

translations.literal: Sons and grandsons of corrupt officials may not be investigated and recommended.
translations.idiomatic: Descendants of officials convicted of corruption are barred from candidacy.

sentences.zh: 杜絕邪偽請託之原。

translations.literal: Block off the source of wickedness, falsity, and pulling strings.
translations.idiomatic: Cut off at the root the traffic in favoritism and fraud.

sentences.zh: 令廉白守道者得信其操。

translations.literal: Cause those who are pure and guard the Way to be able to have faith in their conduct.
translations.idiomatic: Let honest men who hold to principle know their integrity will be trusted.

sentences.zh: 各明守所司，將觀厥後。』

translations.literal: Each clarify and guard what you oversee; I will observe thereafter."
translations.idiomatic: Each of you must be clear in your duties; I shall be watching what follows."

sentences.zh: 九月戊戌，追尊皇祖河間孝王曰孝穆皇，夫人趙氏曰孝穆皇后，皇考蠡吾侯曰孝崇皇。

translations.literal: In the ninth month on wuxu he posthumously honored his imperial grandfather Prince Xiao of Hejian as Emperor Xiaomu, Lady Zhao as Empress Xiaomu, and his imperial father the Marquis of Liyi as Emperor Xiaochong.
translations.idiomatic: On wuxu in the ninth month he posthumously elevated his grandfather, Prince Xiao of Hejian, to the temple name Xiaomu; Lady Zhao became Empress Xiaomu; his father, Marquis of Liyi, was honored as Emperor Xiaochong.

sentences.zh: 冬十月甲午，尊皇母匽氏為孝崇博園貴人。

translations.literal: In winter, the tenth month, on jiawu, he honored his imperial mother of the Yan clan as Honored Lady of the Xiaochong Broad Park.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth month, on jiawu, he raised his mother, Lady Yan, to the title Honored Lady of the Xiaochong park precinct.

sentences.zh: 建和元年春正月辛亥朔，日有食之。

translations.literal: In the first year of Jianhe, spring, the first month, on xinhai the first day of the month, there was a solar eclipse.
translations.idiomatic: In the first year of Jianhe (147), on new year's day, the first of the first month (xinhai), a solar eclipse occurred.

sentences.zh: 詔三公、九卿、校尉各言得失。

translations.literal: He ordered the Three Dukes, the Nine Ministers, and the colonels each to speak of gain and loss.
translations.idiomatic: He ordered the Three Dukes, the Nine Ministers, and the colonels each to memorialize on what was right and wrong in government.

sentences.zh: 戊午，大赦天下。

translations.literal: On wuwu there was a great amnesty for all under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: On wuwu he proclaimed a general amnesty.

sentences.zh: 賜吏更勞一歲；

translations.literal: He bestowed on officials renewal of labor one year;
translations.idiomatic: Officials were granted an extra year's seniority for merit;

sentences.zh: 男子爵，人二級，為父後及三老、孝悌、力田人三級；

translations.literal: for men, noble rank, each person two grades; for heirs of fathers and for the three elders, filial and fraternal exemplars, and champions of agriculture, each person three grades;
translations.idiomatic: commoner men received two ranks in the nobility; heirs, village elders, models of filial piety and brotherly duty, and outstanding farmers received three;

sentences.zh: 鰥、寡、孤、獨、篤癃、貧不能自存者粟，人五斛；

translations.literal: for widowers, widows, orphans, solitaries, the seriously ill and crippled, and the poor unable to support themselves, grain, each person five hu;
translations.idiomatic: widowers, widows, orphans, the childless, the seriously disabled, and the destitute were given grain, five hu per person;

sentences.zh: 貞婦帛，人三匹。

translations.literal: for chaste wives, silk, each person three bolts.
translations.idiomatic: virtuous widows received three bolts of silk each.

sentences.zh: 灾害所傷什四以上，勿收田租；

translations.literal: Where disaster harm reached forty percent or more, do not collect field rent;
translations.idiomatic: Where calamity had destroyed forty percent or more of the crop, field tax was remitted;

sentences.zh: 其不滿者，以實除之。

translations.literal: those not reaching that, according to reality remove it.
translations.idiomatic: where the loss fell short of that fraction, remission was scaled to the actual damage.

sentences.zh: 二月，荊、揚二州人多餓死，遣四府掾分行賑給。

translations.literal: In the second month, in Jing and Yang provinces many people starved to death; he dispatched the clerks of the four offices separately to go and relieve and supply.
translations.idiomatic: In the second month starvation killed many people in Jing and Yang; he sent out staff from the four chief offices to distribute relief by circuit.

sentences.zh: 沛國言黃龍見譙。

translations.literal: Pei commandery reported a yellow dragon appeared at Qiao.
translations.idiomatic: Pei commandery reported a yellow dragon sighting at Qiao.

sentences.zh: 夏四月庚寅，京師地震。

translations.literal: In summer, the fourth month, on gengyin, the capital shook in an earthquake.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth month, on gengyin, the capital was struck by an earthquake.

sentences.zh: 詔大將軍、公、卿、校尉舉賢良方正、能直言極諫者各一人。

translations.literal: He ordered the Grand General, the dukes, ministers, and colonels each to recommend one worthy and good, upright in conduct, able to speak bluntly and remonstrate to the utmost.
translations.idiomatic: He ordered the Grand General, the dukes, ministers, and colonels each to nominate one candidate of outstanding integrity willing to speak truth to power.

sentences.zh: 又命列侯、將、大夫、御史、謁者、千石、六百石、博士、議郎、郎官各上封事，指陳得失。

translations.literal: He also ordered full marquises, generals, grandees, imperial clerks, receptionists, officials of one thousand shi and six hundred shi, erudits, advisory gentlemen, and gentlemen of the court each to submit sealed memorials, pointing out gain and loss.
translations.idiomatic: Marquises, generals, senior officials down to six hundred shi, erudits, advisory gentlemen, and court gentlemen were each told to file sealed memorials on the state of government.

sentences.zh: 又詔大將軍、公、卿、郡、國舉至孝篤行之士各一人。

translations.literal: He further ordered the Grand General, dukes, ministers, commanderies, and kingdoms each to recommend one man of utmost filial piety and solid conduct.
translations.idiomatic: He also told the Grand General, the Three Dukes, ministers, and every commandery and kingdom each to recommend one man famed for filial devotion and steadfast character.

sentences.zh: 壬辰，詔州郡不得迫脅驅逐長吏。

translations.literal: On renchen he ordered that provinces and commanderies must not coerce and drive out senior officials.
translations.idiomatic: On renchen he forbade provincial and commandery authorities to hound senior local officials out of office.

sentences.zh: 長吏臧滿三十萬而不糾舉者，刺史、二千石以縱避為罪。

translations.literal: When a senior official's corruption reaches three hundred thousand cash and is not investigated and reported, the regional inspector and the two-thousand-shi official are guilty of indulgence and evasion.
translations.idiomatic: If a magistrate's bribes reached three hundred thousand cash and his superiors failed to impeach him, the regional inspector and the two-thousand-shi governor were held liable for covering it up.

sentences.zh: 若有擅相假印綬者，與殺人同弃市論。

translations.literal: If there are those who on their own lend one another seals and ribbons, with killing a person the same: judged abandoned in the market.
translations.idiomatic: Anyone who lent official seals and credentials without authority was to be executed in the marketplace like a murderer.

sentences.zh: 丙午，詔郡國繫囚減死罪一等，勿笞。

translations.literal: On bingwu he ordered that in commanderies and kingdoms prisoners bound in fetters, reduce capital crime one degree, do not beat with the bamboo.
translations.idiomatic: On bingwu he ordered commanderies and kingdoms to commute capital sentences by one degree and to waive corporal punishment for those affected.

sentences.zh: 唯謀反大逆，不用此書。

translations.literal: Only plotting rebellion and great treason do not use this document.
translations.idiomatic: The order did not apply to treason or comparable capital crimes.

sentences.zh: 又詔曰：『比起陵塋，彌歷時歲，力役既廣，徒隸尤勤。

translations.literal: Another edict said: "Recently in raising tombs and mounds, it has stretched across years; corvée labor is already broad, and convict laborers are especially toiling."
translations.idiomatic: Another edict ran: "Work on the imperial tombs has dragged on for years, the draft of labor is heavy, and the convict gangs are worn to the bone."

sentences.zh: 頃雨澤不沾，密雲復散，儻或在茲。

translations.literal: Recently rain moisture has not moistened; dense clouds again scatter; perhaps the cause lies here.
translations.idiomatic: Rain has failed to fall though clouds gather and break; perhaps the fault lies here.

sentences.zh: 其令徒作陵者減刑各六月。』

translations.literal: Let it be ordered that convicts working on the tombs have punishment reduced each six months."
translations.idiomatic: Let every convict laboring on the tombs have his sentence shortened by six months."

sentences.zh: 是月，立阜陵王代兄勃遒亭侯便為阜陵王。

translations.literal: That month he established Bian, Marquis of Boqiu Pavilion, elder brother of King Dai of Fuling, as King of Fuling.
translations.idiomatic: That month Liu Bian, Marquis of Boqiu Pavilion and elder brother of King Dai of Fuling, was enthroned as the new King of Fuling.

sentences.zh: 郡國六地裂，水涌井溢。

translations.literal: In six commanderies and kingdoms the earth split; water gushed and wells overflowed.
translations.idiomatic: The earth split in six commanderies and kingdoms; springs burst forth and wells flooded over.

sentences.zh: 芝草生中黃藏府。

translations.literal: Lingzhi fungus grew in the Central Yellow treasury.
translations.idiomatic: Lingzhi sprouted in the imperial treasury known as the Central Yellow storehouse.

sentences.zh: 六月，太尉胡廣罷，大司農杜喬為太尉。

translations.literal: In the sixth month Grand Commandant Hu Guang was dismissed; Minister of Finance Du Qiao became Grand Commandant.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth month Hu Guang was removed as Grand Commandant and Du Qiao, Minister of Finance, took his place.

sentences.zh: 秋七月，勃海王鴻薨，立帝弟蠡吾侯悝為勃海王。

translations.literal: In autumn, the seventh month, the King of Bohai, Hong, died; he established the emperor's younger brother, Marquis of Liyi Kui, as King of Bohai.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh month the King of Bohai, Liu Hong, died; the emperor's younger brother Liu Kui, Marquis of Liyi, was enthroned in Bohai.

sentences.zh: 八月乙未，立皇后梁氏。

translations.literal: In the eighth month on yiwei he established Lady Liang as empress.
translations.idiomatic: On yiwei in the eighth month he took Lady Liang as empress.

sentences.zh: 九月丁卯，京師地震。

translations.literal: In the ninth month on dingmao the capital shook in an earthquake.
translations.idiomatic: On dingmao in the ninth month the capital was shaken by an earthquake.

sentences.zh: 太尉杜喬免。

translations.literal: Grand Commandant Du Qiao was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: Du Qiao was removed from the office of Grand Commandant.

sentences.zh: 冬十月，司徒趙戒為太尉，司空袁湯為司徒，前太尉胡廣為司空。

translations.literal: In winter, the tenth month, Minister of Education Zhao Jie became Grand Commandant, Minister of Works Yuan Tang became Minister of Education, former Grand Commandant Hu Guang became Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth month Zhao Jie rose from Minister of Education to Grand Commandant; Yuan Tang moved from Minister of Works to Minister of Education; Hu Guang, lately Grand Commandant, became Minister of Works.

sentences.zh: 十一月，濟陰言有五色大鳥見於己氏。

translations.literal: In the eleventh month Jiyin reported there was a five-colored great bird seen at Yishi.
translations.idiomatic: In the eleventh month Jiyin commandery reported a large many-colored bird at Yishi.

sentences.zh: 戊午，減天下死罪一等，戍邊。

translations.literal: On wuwu he reduced for all under Heaven capital crime one degree, garrison the frontier.
translations.idiomatic: On wuwu capital sentences throughout the realm were commuted by one degree and the convicts sent to frontier garrison duty.

sentences.zh: 清河劉文反，殺國相射暠，欲立清河王蒜為天子；

translations.literal: Liu Wen of Qinghe rebelled, killed the kingdom chancellor She Hao, intending to establish Liu Suan, King of Qinghe, as Son of Heaven;
translations.idiomatic: Liu Wen of Qinghe rose in revolt, murdered Chancellor She Hao, and meant to set Liu Suan, the King of Qinghe, on the throne;

sentences.zh: 事覺伏誅。

translations.literal: the affair was discovered and they were executed.
translations.idiomatic: the plot was uncovered and they were put to death.

sentences.zh: 蒜坐貶為尉氏候，徙桂陽，自殺。

translations.literal: Suan because of involvement was demoted to Marquis of Weishi, moved to Guiyang, and killed himself.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Suan was stripped of his kingship, reduced to Marquis of Weishi, transferred to Guiyang, and took his own life.

sentences.zh: 前太尉李固、杜喬皆下獄死。

translations.literal: Former Grand Commandants Li Gu and Du Qiao both were cast into prison and died.
translations.idiomatic: The former Grand Commandants Li Gu and Du Qiao were thrown into prison and died there.

sentences.zh: 陳留盜賊李堅自稱皇帝，伏誅。

translations.literal: The bandit of Chenliu, Li Jian, called himself emperor; he submitted to execution.
translations.idiomatic: In Chenliu the outlaw Li Jian declared himself emperor and was put to death.

sentences.zh: 二年春正月甲子，皇帝加元服。

translations.literal: In the second year, spring, the first month, on jiazi, the emperor added the cap of adulthood.
translations.idiomatic: In the second year of Jianhe, on jiazi in the first month, the emperor came of age and received the adult cap.

sentences.zh: 庚午，大赦天下。

translations.literal: On gengwu there was a great amnesty for all under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: On gengwu he proclaimed a general amnesty.

sentences.zh: 賜河間、勃海二王黃金各百斤，彭城諸國王各五十斤；

translations.literal: He bestowed on the two kings of Hejian and Bohai gold each one hundred jin, on the various kings of the Pengcheng domain each fifty jin;
translations.idiomatic: The kings of Hejian and Bohai each received a hundred jin of gold; the kings of the Pengcheng cluster of fiefs received fifty jin each;

sentences.zh: 公主、大將軍、三公、特進、侯、中二千石、二千石、將、大夫、郎吏、從官、四姓及梁、鄧小侯、諸夫人以下帛，各有差。

translations.literal: on princesses, the Grand General, the Three Dukes, specially advanced, marquises, fully two-thousand-shi and two-thousand-shi officials, generals, grandees, gentleman clerks, attendants, the four surname groups and the minor marquises of Liang and Deng, and ladies downward, silk, each according to gradation.
translations.idiomatic: princesses, the Grand General, the Three Dukes, specially advanced nobles, marquises, officials from two thousand shi down through generals, grandees, court gentlemen and attendants, the consort clans and the junior Liang and Deng marquises, and the imperial ladies received silk in graded amounts.

sentences.zh: 年八十以上賜米、酒、肉，九十以上加帛二匹，綿三斤。

translations.literal: Those eighty sui and above were bestowed rice, wine, and meat; those ninety and above additionally two bolts of silk, three jin of floss.
translations.idiomatic: Commoners eighty or older received grain, wine, and meat; those ninety or older received two extra bolts of silk and three jin of cotton.

sentences.zh: 三月戊辰，帝從皇太后幸大將軍梁冀府。

translations.literal: In the third month on wuchen the emperor followed the empress dowager in visiting the mansion of Grand General Liang Ji.
translations.idiomatic: On wuchen in the third month the emperor accompanied the empress dowager on a visit to Grand General Liang Ji's residence.

sentences.zh: 白馬羌寇廣漢屬國，殺長吏，益州刺史率板楯蠻討破之。

translations.literal: The Baima Qiang raided the Guanghan dependent state, killed senior officials; the Inspector of Yi Province led the Shield-board barbarians to attack and defeat them.
translations.idiomatic: Baima Qiang tribesmen attacked the Guanghan dependent state and killed local officials; the inspector of Yi led Shield-board auxiliaries and routed them.

sentences.zh: 夏四月丙子，封帝弟碩為平原王，奉孝崇皇祀。

translations.literal: In summer, the fourth month, on bingzi he enfeoffed the emperor's younger brother Shuo as King of Pingyuan, to serve the sacrifices to Emperor Xiaochong.
translations.idiomatic: On bingzi in the fourth month he created his brother Liu Shuo King of Pingyuan to maintain the temple of Emperor Xiaochong.

sentences.zh: 尊孝崇皇夫人馬氏為孝崇園貴人。

translations.literal: He honored the lady of Emperor Xiaochong, Lady Ma, as Honored Lady of the Xiaochong Park.
translations.idiomatic: Lady Ma, consort of the late Emperor Xiaochong, was raised to Honored Lady of the Xiaochong park precinct.

sentences.zh: 嘉禾生大司農帑藏。

translations.literal: Auspicious grain grew in the Minister of Finance's treasury storehouse.
translations.idiomatic: Twin-eared grain sprouted in the Minister of Finance's granaries—an omen of abundance.

sentences.zh: 五月癸丑，北宮掖廷中德陽殿及左掖門火，車駕移幸南宮。

translations.literal: In the fifth month on guichou, in the Northern Palace's lateral courts Deyang Hall and the left lateral gate caught fire; the imperial residence was transferred to the Southern Palace.
translations.idiomatic: On guichou in the fifth month fire destroyed Deyang Hall and the left lateral gate in the Northern Palace harem; the court removed to the Southern Palace.

sentences.zh: 六月，改清河為甘陵，立安平王得子經侯理為甘陵王。

translations.literal: In the sixth month he changed Qinghe to Ganling and established Jing Marquis Li, son of King De of Anping, as King of Ganling.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth month Qinghe was renamed Ganling, and Liu Li, Marquis of Jing and son of King De of Anping, was installed as King of Ganling.

sentences.zh: 秋七月，京師大水。

translations.literal: In autumn, the seventh month, the capital had great flooding.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh month the capital was inundated by severe floods.

sentences.zh: 河東言木連理。

translations.literal: Hedong reported trees with joined trunks.
translations.idiomatic: Hedong reported the auspicious sign of interfused trees.

sentences.zh: 冬十月，長平陳景自號『黃帝子』，署置官屬，又南頓管伯亦稱『真人』，並圖舉兵，悉伏誅。

translations.literal: In winter, the tenth month, Chen Jing of Changping called himself "Son of the Yellow Emperor," appointed officials and subordinates; also Guan Bo of Nandun called himself "Perfected Man"; together they plotted to raise troops; all were executed.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth month Chen Jing of Changping styled himself the Yellow Emperor's heir and set up a shadow government; Guan Bo of Nandun declared himself a "Perfected Man"; both planned rebellion and were put to death.

sentences.zh: 三年春三月甲申，彭城王定薨。

translations.literal: In the third year, spring, the third month, on jiashen, the King of Pengcheng, Ding, died.
translations.idiomatic: On jiashen in the third month of the third year of Jianhe, Liu Ding, King of Pengcheng, died.

sentences.zh: 夏四月丁卯晦，日有食之。

translations.literal: In summer, the fourth month, on dingmao the last day of the month, there was a solar eclipse.
translations.idiomatic: On the last day of the fourth month, dingmao, a solar eclipse occurred.

sentences.zh: 五月乙亥，詔曰：『蓋聞天生蒸民，不能相理，為之立君，使司牧之。

translations.literal: In the fifth month on yihai an edict said: "I have heard that Heaven produces the multitude of people, and they cannot govern one another; for this reason a ruler is established for them, causing him to oversee and shepherd them."
translations.idiomatic: On yihai in the fifth month an edict declared: "Heaven begat the myriad people, yet they cannot order themselves; a sovereign is set over them to rule and nurture them."

sentences.zh: 君道得於下，則休祥著乎上；

translations.literal: When the ruler's way is obtained below, then auspicious blessing manifests above;
translations.idiomatic: When the ruler's conduct is sound among those below, good omens appear on high;

sentences.zh: 庶事失其序，則咎徵見乎象。

translations.literal: when the multitude of affairs lose their order, then blame-omens appear in the phenomena.
translations.idiomatic: when government falls into disorder, warning signs show themselves in Heaven and Earth.

sentences.zh: 間者，日食毀缺，陽光晦暗，朕祗懼潛思，匪遑啟處。

translations.literal: Recently the sun was eaten and broken and deficient; yang radiance was dark and dim; I reverently fear and secretly ponder, without leisure to rise or sit.
translations.idiomatic: Lately a solar eclipse has dimmed the light of day; I stand in awe and brood night and day, unable to find rest.

sentences.zh: 傳不云乎：「日食修德，月食修刑。

translations.literal: Do the traditions not say: "When the sun is eaten, cultivate virtue; when the moon is eaten, cultivate punishments."
translations.idiomatic: The classics tell us: "An eclipse of the sun calls for moral reform; an eclipse of the moon calls for a review of punishments."

sentences.zh: 」昔孝章帝愍前世禁徙，故建初之元，並蒙恩澤，流徙者使還故郡，沒入者免為庶民。

translations.literal: In former times Emperor Xiaozhang pitied the prohibitions and banishments of previous ages; therefore in the first year of Jianchu all alike received favor and grace: those in flowing exile were caused to return to their former commanderies; those confiscated were released to become commoners.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Zhang once lamented the harsh banishments of earlier reigns; at the opening of the Jianchu era (76–84) he extended a general amnesty—exiles went home, and those enslaved for their crimes were restored to commoner status.

sentences.zh: 先皇德政，可不務乎！

translations.literal: The prior sage emperor's virtuous government—can it not be pursued!
translations.idiomatic: Surely we must follow such an example from our forebears!

sentences.zh: 其自永建元年迄乎今歲，凡諸妖惡，支親從坐，及吏民減死徙邊者，悉歸本郡；

translations.literal: Let it be that from the first year of Yongjian down to this year, for all manner of evil wickedness, collateral kin implicated by association, and officials and commoners whose death was reduced and who were banished to the frontier—all return to their original commanderies;
translations.idiomatic: Therefore, from the first year of Yongjian (126) to the present, everyone sentenced for capital crimes of sorcery or the like, their kin punished by association, and all officials and commoners whose death sentences were commuted to frontier exile, may return to their home commanderies;

sentences.zh: 唯沒入者不從此令。』

translations.literal: only those confiscated do not follow this order.
translations.idiomatic: those who had been confiscated into government servitude are excepted.

sentences.zh: 六月庚子，詔大將軍、三公、特進、侯，其與卿、校尉舉賢良方正、能直言極諫之士各一人。

translations.literal: In the sixth month on gengzi he ordered the Grand General, the Three Dukes, specially advanced, and marquises, together with ministers and colonels, each to recommend one worthy and good, upright in conduct, able to speak bluntly and remonstrate to the utmost.
translations.idiomatic: On gengzi in the sixth month he directed the Grand General, the Three Dukes, specially advanced nobles, and marquises, jointly with the ministers and colonels, each to nominate one man of integrity outspoken enough to remonstrate fearlessly.

sentences.zh: 乙卯，震憲陵寢屋。

translations.literal: On yimao there was a quake; the sleeping hall of Xianling was shaken.
translations.idiomatic: On yimao an earthquake damaged the hall over Emperor Zhang's tomb at Xianling.

sentences.zh: 秋七月庚申，廉縣雨肉。

translations.literal: In autumn, the seventh month, on gengshen, Lian county rained flesh.
translations.idiomatic: On gengshen in the seventh month flesh fell from the sky in Lian county.

sentences.zh: 八月乙丑，有星孛於天市。

translations.literal: In the eighth month on yichou there was a broom star in the Celestial Market.
translations.idiomatic: On yichou in the eighth month a comet appeared in the Celestial Market asterism.

sentences.zh: 京師大水。

translations.literal: The capital had great flooding.
translations.idiomatic: The capital was hit by serious flooding.

sentences.zh: 九月己卯，地震。

translations.literal: In the ninth month on jimao there was an earthquake.
translations.idiomatic: On jimao in the ninth month the earth shook.

sentences.zh: 庚寅，地又震。

translations.literal: On gengyin the earth again shook.
translations.idiomatic: On gengyin there was another tremor.

sentences.zh: 詔死罪以下及亡命者贖，各有差。

translations.literal: He ordered that from capital crime downward and fugitives, ransom, each according to gradation.
translations.idiomatic: He allowed commutation by fine for all crimes short of capital and for fugitives who surrendered, the amounts graded by offense.

sentences.zh: 郡國五山崩。

translations.literal: In five commanderies and kingdoms mountains collapsed.
translations.idiomatic: Landslides struck five commanderies and kingdoms.

sentences.zh: 冬十月，太尉趙戒免。

translations.literal: In winter, the tenth month, Grand Commandant Zhao Jie was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth month Zhao Jie was removed as Grand Commandant.

sentences.zh: 司徒袁湯為太尉，大司農河內張歆為司徒。

translations.literal: Minister of Education Yuan Tang became Grand Commandant; Minister of Finance Zhang Xin of Henei became Minister of Education.
translations.idiomatic: Yuan Tang advanced from Minister of Education to Grand Commandant; Zhang Xin of Henei, Minister of Finance, succeeded him as Minister of Education.

sentences.zh: 十一月甲申，詔曰：『朕攝政失中，灾眚連仍，三光不明，陰陽錯序。

translations.literal: In the eleventh month on jiashen an edict said: "I in holding government have missed the mean; disasters and prodigies follow one after another; the three luminaries are not bright; yin and yang are in disordered sequence."
translations.idiomatic: On jiashen in the eleventh month he issued an edict: "My government has strayed from the right course; calamities pile up one upon another; sun, moon, and stars shine wanly; yin and yang are out of joint."

sentences.zh: 監寐寤歎，疢如疾首。

translations.literal: Awake I sigh in bed; the pain is like disease in the head.
translations.idiomatic: I lie awake sighing, my heart aching as with a splitting headache.

sentences.zh: 今京師廝舍，死者相枕，郡縣阡陌，處處有之，甚違周文掩胔之義。

translations.literal: Now in the capital's hovels the dead pillow one another; in commanderies and counties on paths and lanes, everywhere there are such; this greatly violates the meaning of King Wen of Zhou covering exposed bones.
translations.idiomatic: In the capital the poor quarters are strewn with corpses head to heel; the same sight meets one on country roads throughout the commanderies—far from the compassion King Wen showed when he ordered the unburied dead gathered in.

sentences.zh: 其有家屬而貧無以葬者，給直，人三千，喪主布三匹；

translations.literal: As for those who have family members but are poor and have no means to bury them, grant cash, each person three thousand, to the chief mourner three bolts of cloth;
translations.idiomatic: Where families are too destitute to bury their dead, grant three thousand cash per corpse and three bolts of cloth to the chief mourner;

sentences.zh: 若無親屬，可於官壖地葬之，表識姓名，為設祠祭。

translations.literal: if there are no kin, they may be buried on official vacant land, mark and record the names, and for them set up shrine sacrifice.
translations.idiomatic: where there is no kin, bury them on unused public ground, record each name on a marker, and offer the prescribed sacrifices at the grave.

sentences.zh: 又徙在作部，疾病致醫藥，死亡厚埋藏。

translations.literal: Further, those transferred to the works department: for illness provide medicine; for death give generous burial.
translations.idiomatic: Those reassigned to the convict workshops were to receive medical care when ill and a proper burial when they died.

sentences.zh: 民有不能自振及流移者，稟穀如科。

translations.literal: Among the people those unable to raise themselves and those drifting and migrating, issue grain according to the regulations.
translations.idiomatic: Anyone too destitute to get by, or displaced on the roads, was to be issued grain under the standing rules.

sentences.zh: 州郡檢察，務崇恩施，以康我民。』

translations.literal: Let provinces and commanderies inspect and oversee; strive to enlarge grace and bestowal, in order to bring peace to our people.
translations.idiomatic: Provinces and commanderies were to supervise relief closely, favor generous aid, and so restore the people to health.

sentences.zh: 和平元年春正月甲子，大赦天下，改元和平。

translations.literal: In the first year of Heping, spring, the first month, on jiazi, there was a great amnesty for all under Heaven; the era name was changed to Heping.
translations.idiomatic: On jiazi in the first month of the first year of Heping, 150, he proclaimed a general amnesty and adopted the era name Heping.

sentences.zh: 乙丑，詔曰：『曩者遭家不造，先帝早世。

translations.literal: On yichou an edict said: "Formerly I suffered the misfortune of an unsettled house; the late emperor passed away in youth."
translations.idiomatic: On yichou an edict ran: "In days past Heaven did not smile on the house of Han, and the late emperor was taken while still young."

sentences.zh: 永惟大宗之重，深思嗣續之福，詢謀台輔，稽之兆占。

translations.literal: Forever pondering the weight of the great descent line, deeply thinking the blessing of succession, I inquired and planned with the terrace assistants, examined it in omens and divination.
translations.idiomatic: I weighed the burden of the imperial line, the blessing of an heir, took counsel with the chief ministers, and sought guidance from the oracles.

sentences.zh: 既建明哲，克定統業，天人協和，萬國咸寧。

translations.literal: Having established the wise and perspicacious, able to settle the unified enterprise, Heaven and man were harmonious; the myriad states were all tranquil.
translations.idiomatic: A wise sovereign was enthroned, the succession secured, Heaven and earth were in accord, and the realm knew peace.

sentences.zh: 元服已加，將即委付，而四方盜竊，頗有未靜，故假延臨政，以須安謐。

translations.literal: The cap of adulthood already having been conferred, I was about to hand over authority, yet in the four quarters banditry was still not fully quiet; therefore I prolonged my regency until peace and calm should return.
translations.idiomatic: Once the emperor came of age I meant to hand over power at once, yet banditry in the provinces had not quite subsided, so I prolonged my regency until calm returned.

sentences.zh: 幸賴股肱禦侮之助，殘醜消蕩，民和年稔，普天率土，遐邇洽同。

translations.literal: Fortunately, with the aid of my chief ministers who turned back peril, the remaining wickedness was swept away; the people were at peace and the harvests good; throughout the realm, far and near, all was in accord.
translations.idiomatic: Thanks to the loyal ministers who beat back disorder, the rebels were swept away, the crops ripened, and from the capital to the frontier the land was at one again.

sentences.zh: 遠覽「復子明辟」之義，近慕先姑歸授之法，及今令辰，皇帝稱制。

translations.literal: Far I perused the meaning of "returning the son, the bright ruler"; near I admired the former aunts' method of returning and entrusting; reaching this good hour, the emperor assumes governance.
translations.idiomatic: Taking the ancient precedent of restoring the grown heir to the throne, and following the example of my late predecessors who stepped aside, on this auspicious day the emperor takes the reins in his own name.

sentences.zh: 群公卿士，虔恭爾位，勠力一意，勉同斷金。

translations.literal: Host of dukes, ministers, and gentlemen, reverently respect your posts; join strength with one mind; strive together as severing metal.
translations.idiomatic: You who hold rank under Heaven: keep your offices with devotion, pull together in one purpose, and be as metal cloven in unity.

sentences.zh: 「展也大成」，則所望矣。』

translations.literal: As the ode says, "He unfolds, and there is great completion"—such is my hope.
translations.idiomatic: May the classic line hold true, that he who unfolds his virtue brings great peace—that is what I ask of you.

sentences.zh: 二月，扶風妖賊裴優自稱皇帝，伏誅。

translations.literal: In the second month the sorcerer-bandit of Fufeng, Pei You, called himself emperor; he was executed.
translations.idiomatic: In the second month Pei You of Fufeng, a rebel who styled himself emperor, was put to death.

sentences.zh: 甲寅，皇太后梁氏崩。

translations.literal: On jiayin the Empress Dowager of the Liang clan died.
translations.idiomatic: On jiayin Empress Dowager Liang died.

sentences.zh: 三月，車駕徙幸北宮。

translations.literal: In the third month the carriage and horses moved to favor the Northern Palace.
translations.idiomatic: In the third month the court moved its residence to the Northern Palace.

sentences.zh: 甲午，葬順烈皇后。

translations.literal: On jiawu they buried Empress Shunlie.
translations.idiomatic: On jiawu Empress Shunlie was laid to rest.

sentences.zh: 夏五月庚辰，尊博園匽貴人曰孝崇皇后。

translations.literal: In summer, the fifth month, on gengchen, they honored the Honored Lady Yan of the Broad Park as Empress Xiaochong.
translations.idiomatic: On gengchen in the fifth month Lady Yan of the Broad Park was raised to the title Empress Xiaochong.

sentences.zh: 秋七月，梓潼山崩。

translations.literal: In autumn, the seventh month, in Zitong mountains collapsed.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh month a landslide struck Zitong.

sentences.zh: 冬十一月辛巳，減天下死罪一等，徙邊戍。

translations.literal: In winter, the eleventh month, on xinsi, they reduced for all under Heaven capital crime one degree, moved to frontier garrison.
translations.idiomatic: On xinsi in the eleventh month capital sentences were commuted by one degree and the convicts sent to frontier garrison duty.

sentences.zh: 元嘉元年春正月，京師疾疫，使光祿大夫將醫藥案行。

translations.literal: In the first year of Yuanjia, spring, the first month, in the capital there was pestilence; he sent grandees of the Household bearing medicine to go and inspect.
translations.idiomatic: In the first month of the first year of Yuanjia, 151, plague broke out in the capital; he dispatched Household grandees with medicines to tour the wards and treat the sick.

sentences.zh: 癸酉，大赦天下，改元元嘉。

translations.literal: On guiyou there was a great amnesty for all under Heaven; the era name was changed to Yuanjia.
translations.idiomatic: On guiyou he proclaimed a general amnesty and adopted the era name Yuanjia.

sentences.zh: 二月，九江、廬江大疫。

translations.literal: In the second month in Jiujiang and Lujiang there was great pestilence.
translations.idiomatic: In the second month Jiujiang and Lujiang were swept by a severe epidemic.

sentences.zh: 甲午，河間王建薨。

translations.literal: On jiawu the King of Hejian, Jian, died.
translations.idiomatic: On jiawu Liu Jian, King of Hejian, died.

sentences.zh: 夏四月己丑，安平王得薨。

translations.literal: In summer, the fourth month, on jichou, the King of Anping, De, died.
translations.idiomatic: On jichou in the fourth month Liu De, King of Anping, died.

sentences.zh: 京師旱。

translations.literal: The capital had drought.
translations.idiomatic: Drought gripped the capital.

sentences.zh: 任城、梁國饑，民相食。

translations.literal: Rencheng and Liang state had famine; the people ate one another.
translations.idiomatic: Famine in Rencheng and the state of Liang drove people to cannibalism.

sentences.zh: 司徒張歆罷，光祿勳吳雄為司徒。

translations.literal: Minister of Education Zhang Xin was dismissed; Household Minister Wu Xiong became Minister of Education.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Xin was removed as Minister of Education; Wu Xiong, Household Minister, replaced him.

sentences.zh: 秋七月，武陵蠻叛。

translations.literal: In autumn, the seventh month, the Wuling Man rebelled.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh month the Wuling tribes rose in revolt.

sentences.zh: 冬十月，司空胡廣罷。

translations.literal: In winter, the tenth month, Minister of Works Hu Guang was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth month Hu Guang was dismissed as Minister of Works.

sentences.zh: 十一月辛巳，京師地震。

translations.literal: In the eleventh month on xinsi the capital shook in an earthquake.
translations.idiomatic: On xinsi in the eleventh month the capital was shaken by an earthquake.

sentences.zh: 閏月庚午，任城王崇薨。

translations.literal: In the intercalary month on gengwu the King of Rencheng, Chong, died.
translations.idiomatic: On gengwu in the intercalary month Liu Chong, King of Rencheng, died.

sentences.zh: 太常黃瓊為司空。

translations.literal: Minister of Ceremonies Huang Qiong became Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: Huang Qiong, Minister of Ceremonies, was appointed Minister of Works.

sentences.zh: 二年春正月，西域長史王敬為于窴國所殺。

translations.literal: In the second year, spring, the first month, the Chief Clerk of the Western Regions Wang Jing was killed by the state of Yutian.
translations.idiomatic: In the first month of the second year of Yuanjia Wang Jing, Chief Clerk of the Western Regions, was killed in Yutian.

sentences.zh: 丙辰，京師地震。

translations.literal: On bingchen the capital shook in an earthquake.
translations.idiomatic: On bingchen the capital was struck by an earthquake.

sentences.zh: 夏四月甲寅，孝崇皇后匽氏崩。

translations.literal: In summer, the fourth month, on jiayin, Empress Xiaochong of the Yan clan died.
translations.idiomatic: On jiayin in the fourth month Empress Xiaochong, Lady Yan, died.

sentences.zh: 庚午，常山王豹薨。

translations.literal: On gengwu the King of Changshan, Bao, died.
translations.idiomatic: On gengwu Liu Bao, King of Changshan, died.

sentences.zh: 五月辛卯，葬孝崇皇后于博陵。

translations.literal: In the fifth month on xinmao they buried Empress Xiaochong at Boling.
translations.idiomatic: On xinmao in the fifth month Empress Xiaochong was interred at Boling.

sentences.zh: 秋七月庚辰，日有食之。

translations.literal: In autumn, the seventh month, on gengchen, there was a solar eclipse.
translations.idiomatic: On gengchen in the seventh month a solar eclipse occurred.

sentences.zh: 八月，濟陰言黃龍見句陽，金城言黃龍見允街。

translations.literal: In the eighth month Jiyin reported a yellow dragon seen at Gouyang; Jincheng reported a yellow dragon seen at Yunjie.
translations.idiomatic: In the eighth month Jiyin reported a yellow dragon at Gouyang and Jincheng reported another at Yunjie.

sentences.zh: 冬十月乙亥，京師地震。

translations.literal: In winter, the tenth month, on yihai, the capital shook in an earthquake.
translations.idiomatic: On yihai in the tenth month the capital was shaken by an earthquake.

sentences.zh: 十一月，司空黃瓊免。

translations.literal: In the eleventh month Minister of Works Huang Qiong was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: In the eleventh month Huang Qiong was removed as Minister of Works.

sentences.zh: 十二月，特進趙戒為司空。

translations.literal: In the twelfth month Specially Advanced Zhao Jie became Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: In the twelfth month Zhao Jie, Specially Advanced, was appointed Minister of Works.

sentences.zh: 右北平太守和旻坐臧，下獄死。

translations.literal: The Administrator of Youbeiping, He Min, because of corruption sat in prison and died.
translations.idiomatic: He Min, Administrator of Youbeiping, was imprisoned on a corruption charge and died there.

sentences.zh: 永興元年春二月，張掖言白鹿見。

translations.literal: In the first year of Yongxing, spring, the second month, Zhangye reported a white deer seen.
translations.idiomatic: In the second month of the first year of Yongxing, 153, Zhangye reported the sighting of a white deer.

sentences.zh: 三月丁亥，幸鴻池。

translations.literal: In the third month on dinghai he favored Hong Pool.
translations.idiomatic: On dinghai in the third month he visited Hong Pool.

sentences.zh: 夏五月丙申，大赦天下，改元永興。

translations.literal: In summer, the fifth month, on bingshen, there was a great amnesty for all under Heaven; the era name was changed to Yongxing.
translations.idiomatic: On bingshen in the fifth month he proclaimed a general amnesty and adopted the era name Yongxing.

sentences.zh: 丁酉，濟南王廣薨，無子，國除。

translations.literal: On dingyou the King of Jinan, Guang, died; having no son, the state was abolished.
translations.idiomatic: On dingyou Liu Guang, King of Jinan, died without an heir and the kingdom was abolished.

sentences.zh: 秋七月，郡國三十二蝗。

translations.literal: In autumn, the seventh month, in thirty-two commanderies and kingdoms there were locusts.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh month locusts infested thirty-two commanderies and kingdoms.

sentences.zh: 河水溢。

translations.literal: The Yellow River waters overflowed.
translations.idiomatic: The Yellow River burst its banks.

sentences.zh: 百姓饑窮，流冗道路，至有數十萬戶，冀州尤甚。

translations.literal: The hundred surnames were hungry and destitute, drifting redundant on the roads, reaching to several hundred thousand households; Ji Province was especially severe.
translations.idiomatic: The people starved and wandered the highways by the hundred thousand households; Ji Province suffered worst of all.

sentences.zh: 詔在所賑給乏絕，安慰居業。

translations.literal: He ordered that wherever they were, relieve and supply the exhausted and cut off, comfort and settle those who dwelt and worked.
translations.idiomatic: He ordered local authorities to feed the destitute, comfort refugees, and help those who stayed home to keep their livelihoods.

sentences.zh: 冬十月，太尉袁湯免，太常胡廣為太尉。

translations.literal: In winter, the tenth month, Grand Commandant Yuan Tang was dismissed; Minister of Ceremonies Hu Guang became Grand Commandant.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth month Yuan Tang was removed as Grand Commandant and Hu Guang, Minister of Ceremonies, succeeded him.

sentences.zh: 司徒吳雄罷，司空趙戒免；

translations.literal: Minister of Education Wu Xiong was dismissed; Minister of Works Zhao Jie was dismissed;
translations.idiomatic: Wu Xiong stepped down as Minister of Education and Zhao Jie was dismissed as Minister of Works;

sentences.zh: 以太僕黃瓊為司徒，光祿勲房植為司空。

translations.literal: they appointed Minister Coachman Huang Qiong Minister of Education, Household Minister Fang Zhi Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: Huang Qiong, Minister Coachman, became Minister of Education, and Fang Zhi, Household Minister, became Minister of Works.

sentences.zh: 十一月丁丑，詔減天下死罪一等，徙邊戍。

translations.literal: In the eleventh month on dingchou he ordered reduction for all under Heaven of capital crime one degree, move to frontier garrison.
translations.idiomatic: On dingchou in the eleventh month he ordered capital sentences commuted by one degree and the men sent to frontier garrison.

sentences.zh: 是歲，武陵太守應奉招誘叛蠻，降之。

translations.literal: That year the Administrator of Wuling, Ying Feng, enticed and summoned the rebel Man and made them surrender.
translations.idiomatic: That year Ying Feng, Administrator of Wuling, induced the rebel tribes to submit.

sentences.zh: 二年春正月甲午，大赦天下。

translations.literal: In the second year, spring, the first month, on jiawu, there was a great amnesty for all under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: On jiawu in the first month of the second year of Yongxing he proclaimed a general amnesty.

sentences.zh: 二月辛丑，初聽刺史、二千石行三年喪服。

translations.literal: On xinchou for the first time he permitted regional inspectors and two-thousand-shi officials to practice the three-year mourning garb.
translations.idiomatic: On xinchou he first allowed regional inspectors and two-thousand-shi officials to observe the full three-year mourning for parents.

sentences.zh: 癸卯，京師地震，詔公、卿、校尉舉賢良方正、能直言極諫者各一人。

translations.literal: On guimao the capital shook in an earthquake; he ordered dukes, ministers, and colonels each to recommend one worthy and good, upright in conduct, able to speak bluntly and remonstrate to the utmost.
translations.idiomatic: On guimao an earthquake struck the capital; he ordered the Three Dukes, ministers, and colonels each to nominate one upright man willing to speak frankly.

sentences.zh: 詔曰：『比者星辰謬越，坤靈震動，灾異之降，必不空發。

translations.literal: An edict said: "Recently the stars and constellations have erred and crossed; the earth spirit has shaken; the descent of disaster and prodigy surely does not issue empty."
translations.idiomatic: An edict declared: "Lately the stars have run awry and the earth has trembled; such portents are never sent without cause."

sentences.zh: 勑己修政，庶望有補。

translations.literal: I chastise myself and cultivate government, hoping perhaps there will be remedy.
translations.idiomatic: I must discipline myself and set government aright, in hope of making amends.

sentences.zh: 其輿服制度有逾侈長飾者，皆宜損省。

translations.literal: As for carriage and garment regulations that have excess extravagance and long ornament, all ought to be reduced and cut.
translations.idiomatic: Any sumptuary rules that encourage lavish carriages, dress, or ornament are to be pared back.

sentences.zh: 郡縣務存儉約，申明舊令，如永平故事。』

translations.literal: Commanderies and counties must preserve frugality and restraint, clarify and declare the old orders, as in the Yongping precedent.
translations.idiomatic: Commanderies and counties shall practice austerity, reissue the old regulations, and follow the model of the Yongping era.

sentences.zh: 六月，彭城泗水增長逆流。

translations.literal: In the sixth month in Pengcheng the Si River rose and flowed backward.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth month the Si River near Pengcheng swelled and ran upstream.

sentences.zh: 詔司隸校尉、部刺史曰：『蝗灾為害，水變仍至，五穀不登，人無宿儲。

translations.literal: He ordered the Colonel Director of Retainers and the regional inspectors, saying: "Locust disaster does harm; water prodigies come repeatedly; the five grains do not ripen; people have no stored surplus."
translations.idiomatic: He told the Colonel Director of Retainers and the regional inspectors: "Locusts have ravaged the land, floods keep coming, the harvest fails, and households have no reserves."

sentences.zh: 其令所傷郡國種蕪菁以助人食。

translations.literal: Let it be ordered that in harmed commanderies and kingdoms they plant turnips to help people's food.
translations.idiomatic: Let every stricken commandery and kingdom sow turnips to supplement the food supply.

sentences.zh: 』京師蝗。

translations.literal: In the capital there were locusts.
translations.idiomatic: Locusts also swarmed the capital.

sentences.zh: 東海朐山崩。

translations.literal: In Donghai Mount Qu collapsed.
translations.idiomatic: Mount Qu in Donghai commandery collapsed in a landslide.

sentences.zh: 九月丁卯朔，日有食之。

translations.literal: In the ninth month on dingmao the first day of the month, there was a solar eclipse.
translations.idiomatic: On the first day of the ninth month, dingmao, a solar eclipse occurred.

sentences.zh: 詔曰：『朝政失中，雲漢作旱，川靈湧水，蝗螽孽蔓，殘我百穀，太陽虧光，飢饉薦臻。

translations.literal: An edict said: "Court government misses the mean; the Cloud River makes drought; the river spirit gushes water; locusts and grasshoppers breed and spread, injuring our hundred grains; the great sun loses its radiance; famine comes repeatedly."
translations.idiomatic: An edict ran: "Our policies have gone awry: drought has dried the skies, rivers have burst their banks, locusts and grasshoppers devour the crops, the sun has dimmed, and famine returns again and again."

sentences.zh: 其不被害郡縣，當為饑飢餒者儲。

translations.literal: Those commanderies and counties not harmed ought to store for the hungry and famished.
translations.idiomatic: Commanderies and counties spared by disaster must lay up grain for the starving.

sentences.zh: 天下一家，趣不糜爛，則為國寶。

translations.literal: All under Heaven is one family; if what is gathered does not spoil and waste away, then it is the state's treasure.
translations.idiomatic: The empire is one family: grain stockpiled before it rots is the state's true wealth.

sentences.zh: 其禁郡、國不得賣酒，祠祀裁足。』

translations.literal: Let it be ordered that commanderies and kingdoms must not sell wine; sacrifices and offerings cut to sufficiency.
translations.idiomatic: Commanderies and kingdoms are forbidden to sell wine; sacrifices are to be kept to the bare need.

sentences.zh: 太尉胡廣免，司徒黃瓊為太尉。

translations.literal: Grand Commandant Hu Guang was dismissed; Minister of Education Huang Qiong became Grand Commandant.
translations.idiomatic: Hu Guang was removed as Grand Commandant; Huang Qiong left the Ministry of Education to replace him.

sentences.zh: 閏月，光祿勳尹頌為司徒。

translations.literal: In the intercalary month Household Minister Yin Song became Minister of Education.
translations.idiomatic: In the intercalary month Yin Song, Household Minister, was appointed Minister of Education.

sentences.zh: 減天下死罪一等，徙邊戍。

translations.literal: They reduced for all under Heaven capital crime one degree, moved to frontier garrison.
translations.idiomatic: Capital sentences throughout the realm were commuted by one degree and the convicts banished to the frontier.

sentences.zh: 蜀郡李伯詐稱宗室，當立為『太初皇帝』，伏誅。

translations.literal: Li Bo of Shujun falsely called himself imperial clan and declared he ought to be established as Grand Inception Emperor; he was executed.
translations.idiomatic: Li Bo of Shu pretended to imperial descent and declared himself Grand Inception Emperor; he was executed.

sentences.zh: 冬十一月甲辰，校獵上林苑，遂至函谷關，賜所過道傍年九十以上錢，各有差。

translations.literal: In winter, the eleventh month, on jiachen, he conducted a battue hunt in Shanglin Park, then reached Hangu Pass, bestowing on those by the road passed aged ninety and above money, each according to gradation.
translations.idiomatic: In the eleventh month, on jiachen, he hunted in Shanglin Park and rode as far as Hangu Pass, giving cash in graded amounts to every nonagenarian along the route.

sentences.zh: 太山、琅邪賊公孫舉等反叛，殺長吏。

translations.literal: In Taishan and Langye the bandits Gongsun Ju and others rebelled, killed senior officials.
translations.idiomatic: Gongsun Ju and his followers rebelled in Taishan and Langye and murdered local officials.

sentences.zh: 永壽元年春正月戊申，大赦天下，改元永壽。

translations.literal: In the first year of Yongshou, spring, the first month, on wushen, there was a great amnesty for all under Heaven; the era name was changed to Yongshou.
translations.idiomatic: On wushen in the first month of the first year of Yongshou, 155, he proclaimed a general amnesty and adopted the era name Yongshou.

sentences.zh: 二月，司隸、冀州飢，人相食。

translations.literal: In the second month in the Metropolitan Province and Ji Province there was famine; people ate one another.
translations.idiomatic: In the second month famine in the capital region and Ji Province drove people to cannibalism.

sentences.zh: 勑州郡賑給貧弱。

translations.literal: He ordered provinces and commanderies to relieve and supply the poor and weak.
translations.idiomatic: He ordered the provinces and commanderies to relieve the destitute.

sentences.zh: 若王侯吏民有積穀者，一切貣十分之三，以助稟貸；

translations.literal: If kings, marquises, officials, and commoners have stored grain, all alike lend three tenths, in order to help issue loans;
translations.idiomatic: Princes, marquises, officials, and commoners with grain in store were required to lend three tenths of their holdings for relief distribution;

sentences.zh: 其百姓吏民者，以見錢雇直。

translations.literal: As for commoner households, pay them in cash at a fair rate.
translations.idiomatic: Commoners who surrendered grain from their stores were to be paid in cash at a fair price.

sentences.zh: 王侯須新租乃償。

translations.literal: Kings and marquises must wait for new rent then repay.
translations.idiomatic: Princes and marquises might defer repayment until the new harvest rent came in.

sentences.zh: 夏四月，白烏見齊國。

translations.literal: In summer, the fourth month, a white crow was seen in Qi state.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth month a white crow appeared in the state of Qi.

sentences.zh: 六月，洛水溢，壞鴻德苑。

translations.literal: In the sixth month the Luo River overflowed, destroying Hongde Park.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth month the Luo flooded and wrecked Hongde Park.

sentences.zh: 南陽大水。

translations.literal: Nanyang had great water.
translations.idiomatic: Nanyang was inundated by severe floods.

sentences.zh: 司空房植免，太常韓縯為司空。

translations.literal: Minister of Works Fang Zhi was dismissed; Minister of Ceremonies Han Yan became Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: Fang Zhi was dismissed as Minister of Works; Han Yan, Minister of Ceremonies, took the post.

sentences.zh: 詔太山、琅邪遇賊者，勿收租、賦，復更、筭三年。

translations.literal: He ordered that in Taishan and Langye those who encountered bandits, do not collect rent and levies, remit corvée and poll tax three years.
translations.idiomatic: He ordered that in Taishan and Langye districts hit by banditry no rent or taxes be collected and corvée and poll tax be waived for three years.

sentences.zh: 又詔被水死流失屍骸者，令郡縣鉤求收葬；

translations.literal: He further ordered that for those who died in water and drifted away losing corpse bones, let commanderies and counties hook-seek, collect, and bury;
translations.idiomatic: He also told commanderies and counties to recover and bury corpses washed away in the flood;

sentences.zh: 及所唐突壓溺物故，七歲以上賜錢，人二千。

translations.literal: As for those killed by being crushed or drowned in the panic, if seven sui or older, bestow cash, each person two thousand.
translations.idiomatic: Anyone seven sui or older killed in the stampede or drowning was granted two thousand cash toward funeral expenses.

sentences.zh: 壞敗廬舍，亡失穀食，尤貧者稟，人二斛。』

translations.literal: Ruined and defeated huts and houses, lost grain and food, the especially poor issue grain, each person two hu.
translations.idiomatic: Where homes and grain stores were destroyed, the neediest were issued two hu per person.

sentences.zh: 巴郡、益州郡山崩。

translations.literal: In Bajun and Yizhou commandery mountains collapsed.
translations.idiomatic: Landslides struck Ba commandery and Yizhou commandery.

sentences.zh: 秋七月，初置太山、琅邪都尉官。

translations.literal: In autumn, the seventh month, for the first time they established Taishan and Langye chief commandant offices.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh month chief commandant posts were first created for Taishan and Langye.

sentences.zh: 南匈奴左薁鞬臺耆、且渠伯德等叛，寇美稷，安定屬國都尉張奐討除之。

translations.literal: The Southern Xiongnu left Yujian Taiqi, Quchu Bode, and others rebelled, raided Meiji; the Dependent State Chief Commandant of Anding, Zhang Huan, attacked and eliminated them.
translations.idiomatic: Taiqi of the left Yujian wing and Quchu Bode of the Southern Xiongnu rebelled and raided Meiji; Zhang Huan, chief commandant of the Anding dependent state, destroyed them.

sentences.zh: 二年春正月，初聽中官得行三年服。

translations.literal: In the second year, spring, the first month, for the first time he permitted inner officials to be able to practice the three-year mourning.
translations.idiomatic: In the first month of the second year of Yongshou he first allowed eunuch officials to observe the full three-year mourning for parents.

sentences.zh: 二月甲申，東海王臻薨。

translations.literal: In the second month on jiashen the King of Donghai, Zhen, died.
translations.idiomatic: On jiashen in the second month Liu Zhen, King of Donghai, died.

sentences.zh: 三月，蜀郡屬國夷叛。

translations.literal: In the third month the Yi of Shujun dependent state rebelled.
translations.idiomatic: In the third month the tribesmen of the Shu dependent state rose in rebellion.

sentences.zh: 秋七月，鮮卑寇雲中。

translations.literal: In autumn, the seventh month, the Xianbei raided Yunzhong.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh month Xianbei horsemen raided Yunzhong.

sentences.zh: 太山賊公孫舉等寇青、兗、徐二州，遣中郎將段颎討，破斬之。

translations.literal: The Taishan bandits Gongsun Ju and others raided Qing, Yan, and Xu provinces; he sent General of the Household Duan Jiong to attack them, defeated and beheaded them.
translations.idiomatic: Gongsun Ju’s Taishan rebels swept through Qing, Yan, and Xu; Duan Jiong was sent against them, crushed their forces, and beheaded the leaders.

sentences.zh: 冬十一月，置太官右監丞官。

translations.literal: In winter, the eleventh month, they established the office of Assistant Director of the Grand Provisioner’s Right.
translations.idiomatic: In the eleventh month the court created the post of right assistant supervisor of the Grand Provisioner.

sentences.zh: 十二月，京師地震。

translations.literal: In the twelfth month the capital shook in an earthquake.
translations.idiomatic: In the twelfth month the capital was struck by an earthquake.

sentences.zh: 三年春正月己未，大赦天下。

translations.literal: In the third year, spring, the first month, on jiwei, there was a great amnesty for all under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: On jiwei in the first month of the third year of Yongshou he proclaimed a general amnesty.

sentences.zh: 夏四月，九真蠻夷叛，太守兒式討之，戰歿；

translations.literal: In summer, the fourth month, the Man and Yi of Jiuzhen rebelled; the Administrator Ni Shi attacked them and died in battle;
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth month the tribes of Jiuzhen rose; Administrator Ni Shi marched against them and fell in battle;

sentences.zh: 遣九真都尉魏朗擊破之。

translations.literal: they sent the Chief Commandant of Jiuzhen Wei Lang to strike and defeat them.
translations.idiomatic: Chief Commandant Wei Lang of Jiuzhen was then sent and broke their host.

sentences.zh: 復屯據日南。

translations.literal: They again encamped and held Rinan.
translations.idiomatic: They regrouped and occupied Rinan.

sentences.zh: 閏月庚辰晦，日有食之。

translations.literal: In the intercalary month on gengchen the last day of the month, there was a solar eclipse.
translations.idiomatic: At month’s end, gengchen in the intercalary month, a solar eclipse occurred.

sentences.zh: 六月，初以小黃門為守宮令，置冗從右僕射官。

translations.literal: In the sixth month, for the first time they used a junior yellow gate as Keeper of the Palace and established the office of supernumerary attendant right vice director.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth month a junior yellow gate was first appointed Keeper of the Palace, and the post of supernumerary attendant right vice director was created.

sentences.zh: 京師蝗。

translations.literal: In the capital there were locusts.
translations.idiomatic: Locusts swarmed the capital.

sentences.zh: 秋七月，河東地裂。

translations.literal: In autumn, the seventh month, in Hedong the earth split.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh month the earth split open in Hedong.

sentences.zh: 冬十一月，司徒尹頌薨。

translations.literal: In winter, the eleventh month, Minister of Education Yin Song died.
translations.idiomatic: In the eleventh month Yin Song, Minister of Education, died.

sentences.zh: 長沙蠻叛，寇益陽。

translations.literal: The Changsha Man rebelled and raided Yiyang.
translations.idiomatic: Changsha tribesmen rose and plundered Yiyang.

sentences.zh: 司空韓縯為司徒，太常北海孫朗為司空。

translations.literal: Minister of Works Han Yan became Minister of Education; Minister of Ceremonies Sun Lang of Beihai became Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: Han Yan moved from Minister of Works to Minister of Education; Sun Lang of Beihai, Minister of Ceremonies, became Minister of Works.

sentences.zh: 延熹元年春三月己酉，初置鴻德苑令。

translations.literal: In the first year of Yanxi, spring, the third month, on jiyou, for the first time they established the Keeper of Hongde Park.
translations.idiomatic: On jiyou in the third month of the first year of Yanxi, 158, the office of keeper of Hongde Park was first instituted.

sentences.zh: 夏五月己酉，大會公卿以下，賞賜各有差。

translations.literal: In summer, the fifth month, on jiyou, they held a great assembly from the dukes downward; bestowals were graded by rank.
translations.idiomatic: On jiyou in the fifth month he held a great audience for the dukes and all below and distributed gifts by rank.

sentences.zh: 甲戌晦，日有食之。

translations.literal: On jiaxu the last day of the month, there was a solar eclipse.
translations.idiomatic: On the last day of the month, jiaxu, a solar eclipse occurred.

sentences.zh: 京師蝗。

translations.literal: In the capital there were locusts.
translations.idiomatic: Locusts again infested the capital.

sentences.zh: 六月戊寅，大赦天下，改元延熹。

translations.literal: In the sixth month on wuyin there was a great amnesty for all under Heaven; the era name was changed to Yanxi.
translations.idiomatic: On wuyin in the sixth month he proclaimed a general amnesty and adopted the era name Yanxi.

sentences.zh: 丙戌，分中山置博陵郡，以奉孝崇皇園陵。

translations.literal: On bingxu they divided off Zhongshan to establish Boling commandery, to serve the park tombs of Emperor Xiaochong.
translations.idiomatic: On bingxu Boling commandery was carved out of Zhongshan to maintain the tombs of Emperor Xiaochong.

sentences.zh: 大雩。

translations.literal: They held the great rain prayer.
translations.idiomatic: The court performed the great rain ceremony.

sentences.zh: 秋七月己巳，雲陽地裂。

translations.literal: In autumn, the seventh month, on jisi, in Yunyang the earth split.
translations.idiomatic: On jisi in the seventh month the earth split at Yunyang.

sentences.zh: 甲子，太尉黃瓊免，太常胡廣為太尉。

translations.literal: On jiazi Grand Commandant Huang Qiong was dismissed; Minister of Ceremonies Hu Guang became Grand Commandant.
translations.idiomatic: On jiazi Huang Qiong was removed as Grand Commandant and Hu Guang, Minister of Ceremonies, replaced him.

sentences.zh: 冬十月，校獵廣成，遂幸上林苑。

translations.literal: In winter, the tenth month, he conducted a battue hunt at Guangcheng, then favored Shanglin Park.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth month he hunted at Guangcheng and then visited Shanglin Park.

sentences.zh: 十二月，鮮卑寇邊，使匈奴中郎將張奐率南單于擊破之。

translations.literal: In the twelfth month the Xianbei raided the frontier; he sent the General of the Household for Dependent States Zhang Huan to lead the Southern Shanyu and strike and defeat them.
translations.idiomatic: In the twelfth month Xianbei raiders struck the border; Zhang Huan, General of the Household for Dependent States, led the Southern Shanyu and routed them.

sentences.zh: 二年春二月，鮮卑寇鴈門。

translations.literal: In the second year, spring, the second month, the Xianbei raided Yanmen.
translations.idiomatic: In the second month of the second year of Yanxi the Xianbei attacked Yanmen.

sentences.zh: 己亥，阜陵王便薨。

translations.literal: On jihai the King of Fuling, Bian, died.
translations.idiomatic: On jihai Liu Bian, King of Fuling, died.

sentences.zh: 蜀郡夷寇蠶陵，殺縣令。

translations.literal: The Yi of Shujun raided Canling and killed the county magistrate.
translations.idiomatic: Tribesmen of Shu commandery attacked Canling and slew the magistrate.

sentences.zh: 三月，復斷刺史、二千石行三年喪。

translations.literal: In the third month they again cut off regional inspectors and two-thousand-shi officials from practicing the three-year mourning.
translations.idiomatic: In the third month the court again forbade regional inspectors and two-thousand-shi officials to observe the full three-year mourning.

sentences.zh: 夏，京師雨水。

translations.literal: In summer, in the capital there was rain water.
translations.idiomatic: That summer the capital saw prolonged rains.

sentences.zh: 六月，鮮卑寇遼東。

translations.literal: In the sixth month the Xianbei raided Liaodong.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth month Xianbei horsemen raided Liaodong.

sentences.zh: 秋七月，初造顯陽苑，置丞。

translations.literal: In autumn, the seventh month, for the first time they built Xianyang Park and established an assistant.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh month work began on Xianyang Park, with an assistant keeper appointed.

sentences.zh: 丙午，皇后梁氏崩。

translations.literal: On bingwu Empress of the Liang clan died.
translations.idiomatic: On bingwu Empress Liang died.

sentences.zh: 乙丑，葬懿獻皇后于懿陵。

translations.literal: On yichou they buried Empress Yixian at Yiling.
translations.idiomatic: On yichou Empress Yixian was interred at Yiling.

sentences.zh: 大將軍梁冀謀為亂。

translations.literal: Grand General Liang Ji plotted rebellion.
translations.idiomatic: Grand General Liang Ji plotted a coup.

sentences.zh: 八月丁丑，帝御前殿，詔司隸校尉張彪將兵圍冀第，收大將軍印綬，冀與妻皆自殺。

translations.literal: In the eighth month on dingchou the emperor took the front hall; he ordered Colonel Director of Retainers Zhang Biao to lead troops and surround Liang Ji’s mansion, seize the grand general’s seal and ribbon; Liang Ji and his wife both killed themselves.
translations.idiomatic: On dingchou in the eighth month the emperor sat in the front hall and ordered Zhang Biao, Colonel Director of Retainers, to surround Liang Ji’s residence with troops, seize the seals of office, whereupon Liang Ji and his wife took their own lives.

sentences.zh: 衛尉梁淑、河南尹梁胤、屯騎校尉梁讓、越騎校尉梁忠、長水校尉梁戟等，及中外宗親數十人，皆伏誅。

translations.literal: Commandant of the Guards Liang Shu, Governor of Henan Liang Yin, Colonel of Garrison Cavalry Liang Rang, Colonel of Agile Cavalry Liang Zhong, Colonel of the Long River Liang Ji, and others, together with inner and outer kin by marriage numbering several tens, all were executed.
translations.idiomatic: Liang Shu, Commandant of the Guards, Liang Yin, Governor of Henan, and the Liang clan colonels of the several cavalry guards, with dozens of kinsmen inside and outside the court, were all put to death.

sentences.zh: 太尉胡廣坐免。

translations.literal: Grand Commandant Hu Guang because of involvement was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: Hu Guang was dismissed as Grand Commandant for his ties to the Liang faction.

sentences.zh: 司徒韓縯、司空孫朗下獄。

translations.literal: Minister of Education Han Yan and Minister of Works Sun Lang were cast into prison.
translations.idiomatic: Han Yan, Minister of Education, and Sun Lang, Minister of Works, were thrown into prison.

sentences.zh: 壬午，立皇后鄧氏，追廢懿陵為貴人冢。

translations.literal: On renwu he established Empress of the Deng clan; he posthumously demoted Yiling to a tomb mound for an honored lady.
translations.idiomatic: On renwu he took Lady Deng as empress and reduced Empress Liang’s Yiling tomb to the burial mound of an honored lady.

sentences.zh: 詔曰：『梁冀奸暴，濁亂王室。

translations.literal: An edict said: "Liang Ji was treacherous and violent; he befouled and threw into disorder the royal house."
translations.idiomatic: An edict declared: "Liang Ji was treacherous and cruel and brought chaos to the imperial house."

sentences.zh: 孝質皇帝聰敏早茂，冀心懷忌畏，私行殺毒。

translations.literal: The Filial and Luminous Emperor was clever and keen and early flourished; in Ji’s heart he harbored jealousy and fear, and in private carried out killing poison.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Zhi was bright and precocious; Liang Ji feared him and had him murdered in secret.

sentences.zh: 永樂太后親尊莫二，冀又遏絕，禁還京師，使朕離母子之愛，隔顧復之恩。

translations.literal: The Yongle Empress Dowager in closeness and honor had no second; Ji again blocked and cut her off, forbidding return to the capital, causing me to be separated from mother–child love, cutting off the grace of looking back and again nurturing.
translations.idiomatic: The Dowager of Yongle stood second to none in kinship and rank; Liang Ji cut her off from the court and barred her return to the capital, severing me from a mother’s love and the care I owed her.

sentences.zh: 禍害深大，罪釁日滋。

translations.literal: The harm and injury were deep and great; guilt and offense daily increased.
translations.idiomatic: The harm he did was grave and his crimes mounted by the day.

sentences.zh: 賴宗廟之靈，及中常侍單超、徐璜、具瑗、左悺、唐衡、尚書令尹勳等激憤建策，內外協同，漏刻之間，桀逆梟夷。

translations.literal: Relying on the numen of the ancestral temples, and on Palace Attendants Shan Chao, Xu Huang, Ju Yuan, Zuo Guan, Tang Heng, and Prefect of Masters of Writing Yin Xun and others, who in indignation devised the plan, inner and outer cooperated; within the space of a water-clock’s drip, the Jie-like rebel was beheaded and exterminated.
translations.idiomatic: Through the spirits of the ancestors and the resolve of the eunuch advisers Shan Chao, Xu Huang, Ju Yuan, Zuo Guan, Tang Heng, and Yin Xun, Prefect of Masters of Writing, court and camp acted as one, and within moments the traitor was destroyed.

sentences.zh: 斯誠社稷之佑，臣下之力，宜班慶賞，以酬忠勳。

translations.literal: This is truly the blessing of the altars of soil and grain and the strength of ministers; it is fitting to distribute celebratory rewards in order to repay loyal merit.
translations.idiomatic: This was Heaven’s blessing on the dynasty and the ministers’ doing; rewards should go forth to repay their loyal service.

sentences.zh: 其封超等五人為縣侯，勛等七人為亭侯。

translations.literal: Let Chao and the five others each be enfeoffed as full marquises of a county, Xun and the seven others each as marquises of a pavilion.
translations.idiomatic: Shan Chao and his four companions were each enfeoffed as county marquises; Yin Xun and six others each as pavilion marquises.

sentences.zh: 』於是舊故恩私，多受封爵。

translations.literal: Thereupon old ties of private favor mostly received enfeoffments and noble ranks.
translations.idiomatic: Many who had long enjoyed private favor with the Liang were now showered with titles.

sentences.zh: 大司農黃瓊為太尉，光祿大夫中山祝恬為司徒，大鴻臚梁國盛允為司空。

translations.literal: Minister of Finance Huang Qiong became Grand Commandant; Grandee of the Household Zhu Tian of Zhongshan became Minister of Education; Grand Herald Sheng Yun of Liang became Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: Huang Qiong became Grand Commandant; Zhu Tian of Zhongshan, Grandee of the Household, became Minister of Education; Sheng Yun of Liang, Grand Herald, became Minister of Works.

sentences.zh: 初置秘書監官。

translations.literal: For the first time they established the office of Director of the Palace Library.
translations.idiomatic: The post of director of the Palace Library was created for the first time.

sentences.zh: 冬十月壬申，行幸長安。

translations.literal: In winter, the tenth month, on renshen, he traveled and favored Chang’an.
translations.idiomatic: On renshen in the tenth month he journeyed to Chang’an.

sentences.zh: 乙酉，幸未中宮。

translations.literal: On yiyou he favored Weizhong Palace.
translations.idiomatic: On yiyou he visited Weiyang Palace in Chang’an.

sentences.zh: 甲午，祠高廟。

translations.literal: On jiawu he sacrificed at the shrine of Gaozu.
translations.idiomatic: On jiawu he offered sacrifice at Gaozu’s temple.

sentences.zh: 十一月庚子，遂有事十一陵。

translations.literal: In the eleventh month on gengzi he then performed the rites at the eleven tombs.
translations.idiomatic: On gengzi in the eleventh month he performed the imperial rites at the eleven Western Han tombs.

sentences.zh: 壬寅，中常侍單超為車騎將軍。

translations.literal: On renyin Palace Attendant Shan Chao became General of Chariots and Cavalry.
translations.idiomatic: On renyin Shan Chao, a palace attendant, was made General of Chariots and Cavalry.

sentences.zh: 十二月己巳，至自長安，賜長安民粟人十斛，園陵人五斛，行所過縣三斛。

translations.literal: In the twelfth month on jisi he arrived from Chang’an; he bestowed on the people of Chang’an grain ten hu per person, on the park-tomb people five hu, on counties passed through in traveling three hu.
translations.idiomatic: On jisi in the twelfth month he returned from Chang’an, giving the capital’s people ten hu of grain each, five hu to those who tended the tombs, and three hu to each county along his route.

sentences.zh: 燒當等八種羌叛，寇隴右，護羌校尉段颎追擊於羅亭，破之。

translations.literal: The eight tribes of Qiang including Shao Dang rebelled and raided Longyou; Colonel Protector of the Qiang Duan Jiong pursued and attacked them at Luoting and defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: Eight Qiang tribes led by Shao Dang rebelled in Longyou; Duan Jiong, Colonel Protector of the Qiang, overtook them at Luoting and broke their power.

sentences.zh: 天竺國來獻。

translations.literal: The state of Tianzhu came to present tribute.
translations.idiomatic: Envoys from India arrived with tribute.

sentences.zh: 三年春正月丙申，大赦天下。

translations.literal: In the third year, spring, the first month, on bingshen, there was a great amnesty for all under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: On bingshen in the first month of the third year of Yanxi he proclaimed a general amnesty.

sentences.zh: 丙午，車騎將軍單超薨。

translations.literal: On bingwu General of Chariots and Cavalry Shan Chao died.
translations.idiomatic: On bingwu Shan Chao, General of Chariots and Cavalry, died.

sentences.zh: 閏月，燒何羌叛，寇張掖，護羌校尉段颎追擊於積石，大破之。

translations.literal: In the intercalary month the Shao He Qiang rebelled and raided Zhangye; Colonel Protector of the Qiang Duan Jiong pursued and attacked them at Jishi and greatly defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: In the intercalary month the Shao He Qiang rose against Zhangye; Duan Jiong ran them down at Jishi and won a crushing victory.

sentences.zh: 白馬令李雲坐直諫，下獄死。

translations.literal: Magistrate of Baima Li Yun because of blunt remonstrance sat in prison and died.
translations.idiomatic: Li Yun, magistrate of Baima, was jailed for outspoken criticism and died there.

sentences.zh: 夏四月，上郡言甘露降。

translations.literal: In summer, the fourth month, Shangjun reported sweet dew descending.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth month Shangjun reported that sweet dew had fallen.

sentences.zh: 五月甲戌，漢中山崩。

translations.literal: In the fifth month on jiaxu Mount Hanzhong collapsed.
translations.idiomatic: On jiaxu in the fifth month a mountain at Hanzhong collapsed in a landslide.

sentences.zh: 六月辛丑，司徒祝恬薨。

translations.literal: In the sixth month on xinchou Minister of Education Zhu Tian died.
translations.idiomatic: On xinchou in the sixth month Zhu Tian, Minister of Education, died.

sentences.zh: 秋七月，司空盛允為司徒，太常虞放為司空。

translations.literal: In autumn, the seventh month, Minister of Works Sheng Yun became Minister of Education; Minister of Ceremonies Yu Fang became Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh month Sheng Yun advanced from Minister of Works to Minister of Education; Yu Fang, Minister of Ceremonies, became Minister of Works.

sentences.zh: 長沙蠻寇郡界。

translations.literal: The Changsha Man raided the commandery borders.
translations.idiomatic: Changsha tribesmen raided the frontier of the commandery.

sentences.zh: 九月，太山、琅邪賊勞丙等復叛，寇掠百姓。

translations.literal: In the ninth month the Taishan and Langye bandits Lao Bing and others again rebelled, raiding and plundering the hundred surnames.
translations.idiomatic: In the ninth month Lao Bing and other bandits in Taishan and Langye rose again and plundered the populace.

sentences.zh: 遣御史中丞趙某持節督州郡討之。

translations.literal: They sent the Grandee Secretary of the Household Zhao Mou bearing the staff of authority to supervise provinces and commanderies in attacking them.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Mou, Grandee Secretary of the Household, was sent with credential staff to direct provincial forces against the rebels.

sentences.zh: 丁亥，詔無事之官權絕奉，豐年如故。

translations.literal: On dinghai he ordered that officials without duties temporarily cut off salary; in abundant years as before.
translations.idiomatic: On dinghai he ordered idle offices to forgo stipends for the time being, resuming full pay only in a bumper year.

sentences.zh: 冬十一月，日南蠻賊率眾詣郡降。

translations.literal: In winter, the eleventh month, the Rinan Man bandits led their masses to the commandery and surrendered.
translations.idiomatic: In the eleventh month Rinan rebels brought their followers to the commandery seat and submitted.

sentences.zh: 勒姐羌圍允街，段颎擊破之。

translations.literal: The Lejie Qiang besieged Yunjie; Duan Jiong struck and defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: The Lejie Qiang surrounded Yunjie; Duan Jiong relieved the town and routed them.

sentences.zh: 太山賊叔孫無忌攻殺都尉侯章。

translations.literal: The Taishan bandit Shusun Wuji attacked and killed Chief Commandant Hou Zhang.
translations.idiomatic: The Taishan rebel Shusun Wuji slew Chief Commandant Hou Zhang.
