sentences.zh: 十二月，遣中郎將宗資討破之。

translations.literal: In the twelfth month he sent General of the Household Zong Zi to attack and defeat them.
translations.idiomatic: In the twelfth month Zong Zi, General of the Household, was sent and crushed the bandits.

sentences.zh: 武陵蠻寇江陵，車騎將軍馮緄討，皆降散。

translations.literal: The Wuling Man raided Jiangling; General of Chariots and Cavalry Feng Gun attacked them; all surrendered or scattered.
translations.idiomatic: Wuling tribesmen struck Jiangling; Feng Gun, General of Chariots and Cavalry, campaigned until they dispersed or yielded.

sentences.zh: 荊州刺史度尚討長沙蠻，平之。

translations.literal: Inspector of Jing Province Du Shang attacked the Changsha Man and pacified them.
translations.idiomatic: Du Shang, inspector of Jing, subdued the Changsha tribesmen.

sentences.zh: 四年春正月辛酉，南宮嘉德殿火。

translations.literal: In the fourth year, spring, the first month, on xinyou, Jiade Hall in the Southern Palace caught fire.
translations.idiomatic: On xinyou in the first month of the fourth year of Yanxi fire broke out in Jiade Hall of the Southern Palace.

sentences.zh: 戊子，丙署火。

translations.literal: On wuzi the Bing office caught fire.
translations.idiomatic: On wuzi fire destroyed the Bing office.

sentences.zh: 大疫。

translations.literal: There was great pestilence.
translations.idiomatic: A great epidemic raged.

sentences.zh: 二月壬辰，武庫火。

translations.literal: In the second month on renchen the armory caught fire.
translations.idiomatic: On renchen in the second month the imperial armory burned.

sentences.zh: 司徒盛允免，大司農种暠為司徒。

translations.literal: Minister of Education Sheng Yun was dismissed; Minister of Finance Zhong Hao became Minister of Education.
translations.idiomatic: Sheng Yun was removed as Minister of Education; Zhong Hao, Minister of Finance, took the post.

sentences.zh: 三月，省冗從右僕射官。

translations.literal: In the third month they abolished the office of supernumerary attendant right vice director.
translations.idiomatic: In the third month the supernumerary attendant right vice director was abolished.

sentences.zh: 太尉黃瓊免。

translations.literal: Grand Commandant Huang Qiong was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: Huang Qiong was dismissed as Grand Commandant.

sentences.zh: 夏四月，太常劉矩為太尉。

translations.literal: In summer, the fourth month, Minister of Ceremonies Liu Ju became Grand Commandant.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth month Liu Ju, Minister of Ceremonies, was appointed Grand Commandant.

sentences.zh: 甲寅，封河間王開子博為任城王。

translations.literal: On jiayin he enfeoffed Bo, son of the King of Hejian, Kai, as King of Rencheng.
translations.idiomatic: On jiayin Liu Bo, son of Liu Kai, King of Hejian, was created King of Rencheng.

sentences.zh: 五月辛酉，有星孛於心。

translations.literal: In the fifth month on xinyou there was a broom star in the Heart asterism.
translations.idiomatic: On xinyou in the fifth month a comet appeared in the Heart mansion.

sentences.zh: 丁卯，原陵長壽門火。

translations.literal: On dingmao the Longevity Gate of Yuanling caught fire.
translations.idiomatic: On dingmao fire broke out at the Longevity Gate of Emperor Guangwu’s tomb.

sentences.zh: 己卯，京師雨雹。

translations.literal: On jimao in the capital there fell hail.
translations.idiomatic: On jimao a hailstorm struck the capital.

sentences.zh: 六月，京兆、扶風及涼州地震。

translations.literal: In the sixth month Jingzhao, Fufeng, and Liang Province shook in an earthquake.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth month earthquakes struck Jingzhao, Fufeng, and Liang Province.

sentences.zh: 庚子，岱山及博尤來山並穨裂。

translations.literal: On gengzi Mount Dai and Mount Youlai near Bo together collapsed and split.
translations.idiomatic: On gengzi Mount Tai and Mount Youlai beside Bo both gave way in massive slides.

sentences.zh: 己酉，大赦天下。

translations.literal: On jiyou there was a great amnesty for all under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: On jiyou he proclaimed a general amnesty.

sentences.zh: 司空虞放免，前太尉黃瓊為司空。

translations.literal: Minister of Works Yu Fang was dismissed; former Grand Commandant Huang Qiong became Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: Yu Fang was removed as Minister of Works; Huang Qiong, lately Grand Commandant, returned to office as Minister of Works.

sentences.zh: 犍為屬國夷寇鈔百姓，益州刺史山昱擊破之。

translations.literal: The Yi of the Qianwei dependent state raided and plundered the hundred surnames; Inspector of Yi Province Shan Yu attacked and defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: Tribesmen of the Qianwei dependent state plundered the people; Shan Yu, inspector of Yi, defeated them.

sentences.zh: 零吾羌與先零諸種並叛，寇三輔。

translations.literal: The Lingwu Qiang together with the Xianling tribes all rebelled and raided the three adjunct capitals.
translations.idiomatic: The Lingwu Qiang and the Xianling confederation rose together and raided the capital region.

sentences.zh: 秋七月，京師雩。

translations.literal: In autumn, the seventh month, in the capital they held the rain prayer.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh month the court held the great rain ceremony in the capital.

sentences.zh: 減公卿以下奉，貣王侯半租。

translations.literal: They reduced salary from dukes and ministers downward, and lent kings and marquises half their rent.
translations.idiomatic: Salaries were cut for the Three Dukes down through the ranks, and princes and marquises were made to lend half their rent income to the treasury.

sentences.zh: 占賣關內侯、虎賁、羽林、緹騎營士、五大夫錢各有差。

translations.literal: They assessed sale of secondary marquis within the passes, tiger guards, feathered forest, cavalry escort camp soldiers, and fifth-grade grandee titles, money each according to gradation.
translations.idiomatic: Ranks of secondary marquis within the passes, tiger guard, feathered forest, escort cavalry, and fifth-grade grandee were put up for purchase at graded prices.

sentences.zh: 九月，司空黃瓊免，大鴻臚劉寵為司空。

translations.literal: In the ninth month Minister of Works Huang Qiong was dismissed; Grand Herald Liu Chong became Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: In the ninth month Huang Qiong left the Ministry of Works; Liu Chong, Grand Herald, succeeded him.

sentences.zh: 冬十月，天竺國來獻。

translations.literal: In winter, the tenth month, the state of Tianzhu came to present tribute.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth month another embassy arrived from India with tribute.

sentences.zh: 南陽黃武與襄城惠得、昆陽樂季訞言相署，皆伏誅。

translations.literal: Huang Wu of Nanyang with Huide of Xiangcheng and Yue Ji of Kunyang falsely accused one another in office titles; all were executed.
translations.idiomatic: Huang Wu of Nanyang, Huide of Xiangcheng, and Yue Ji of Kunyang forged titles for one another; all three were put to death.

sentences.zh: 先零沈氐羌與諸種羌寇幷、涼二州，十一月，中郎將皇甫規擊破之。

translations.literal: The Xianling and Shenshi Qiang together with Qiang of various tribes raided Bing and Liang provinces; in the eleventh month General of the Household Huangfu Gui attacked and defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: Xianling and Shenshi Qiang and allied tribes struck Bing and Liang; in the eleventh month Huangfu Gui routed them.

sentences.zh: 十二月，夫餘王遣使來獻。

translations.literal: In the twelfth month the king of Puyu sent envoys to present tribute.
translations.idiomatic: In the twelfth month the king of Puyu sent tribute to the court.

sentences.zh: 五年春正月，省太官右監丞。

translations.literal: In the fifth year of Yanxi, spring, the first month, they abolished the right assistant supervisor of the Grand Provisioner.
translations.idiomatic: In the first month of the fifth year of Yanxi the post of right assistant supervisor of the Grand Provisioner was abolished.

sentences.zh: 壬午，南宮丙署火。

translations.literal: On renwu the Bing office in the Southern Palace caught fire.
translations.idiomatic: On renwu fire broke out in the Bing office of the Southern Palace.

sentences.zh: 三月，沈氐羌寇張掖、酒泉。

translations.literal: In the third month the Shenshi Qiang raided Zhangye and Jiuquan.
translations.idiomatic: In the third month Shenshi Qiang horsemen struck Zhangye and Jiuquan.

sentences.zh: 壬午，濟北王次薨。

translations.literal: On renwu the King of Jibei, Ci, died.
translations.idiomatic: On renwu Liu Ci, King of Jibei, died.

sentences.zh: 夏四月，長沙賊起，寇桂陽、蒼梧。

translations.literal: In summer, the fourth month, Changsha bandits rose and raided Guiyang and Cangwu.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth month rebels from Changsha rose and plundered Guiyang and Cangwu.

sentences.zh: 驚馬逸象突入宮殿。

translations.literal: Startled horses and runaway elephants burst into the palace halls.
translations.idiomatic: Panicked horses and a loose elephant charged into the palace.

sentences.zh: 乙丑，恭陵東闕火。

translations.literal: On yichou the east gate tower of Gongling caught fire.
translations.idiomatic: On yichou fire destroyed the east gate tower at Emperor An’s Gongling.

sentences.zh: 戊辰，虎賁掖門火。

translations.literal: On wuchen the tiger guard lateral gate caught fire.
translations.idiomatic: On wuchen the tiger guard side gate burned.

sentences.zh: 己巳。

translations.literal: It was the day jisi.
translations.idiomatic: The day was jisi.

sentences.zh: 太學西門自壞。

translations.literal: The west gate of the Imperial Academy collapsed of itself.
translations.idiomatic: The Imperial Academy’s west gate fell in without apparent cause.

sentences.zh: 五月，康陵園寑火。

translations.literal: In the fifth month the sleeping hall of Kangling Park caught fire.
translations.idiomatic: In the fifth month fire damaged the hall at Emperor Shun’s Kangling.

sentences.zh: 長沙、零陵賊起，攻桂陽、蒼梧、南海、交阯，遣御史中丞盛入脩督州郡討之，不克。

translations.literal: Bandits from Changsha and Lingling rose, attacked Guiyang, Cangwu, Nanhai, and Jiaozhi; he sent Grandee Secretary of the Household Sheng Xiu to supervise provinces and commanderies in attacking them; they did not overcome.
translations.idiomatic: Rebels from Changsha and Lingling overran the south; Grandee Secretary Sheng Xiu led provincial forces against them without success.

sentences.zh: 乙亥，京師地震。

translations.literal: On yihai the capital shook in an earthquake.
translations.idiomatic: On yihai an earthquake struck the capital.

sentences.zh: 詔公、卿各上封事。

translations.literal: He ordered dukes and ministers each to submit sealed memorials.
translations.idiomatic: He ordered the Three Dukes and ministers each to file sealed advice.

sentences.zh: 甲申，中藏府承祿署火。

translations.literal: On jiashen the Chenglu office of the central treasury caught fire.
translations.idiomatic: On jiashen fire broke out in the Chenglu office of the imperial treasury.

sentences.zh: 秋七月己未，南宮承善闥火。

translations.literal: In autumn, the seventh month, on jiwei, the Chengshan gate tower of the Southern Palace caught fire.
translations.idiomatic: On jiwei in the seventh month the Chengshan gate of the Southern Palace burned.

sentences.zh: 鳥吾羌寇漢陽、隴西、金城，諸郡兵討破之。

translations.literal: The Wuwu Qiang raided Hanyang, Longxi, and Jincheng; troops of the commanderies attacked and defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: The Wuwu Qiang struck Hanyang, Longxi, and Jincheng; combined commandery forces beat them back.

sentences.zh: 八月庚子，詔減虎賁、羽林住寺不任事者半奉，勿與冬衣；

translations.literal: In the eighth month on gengzi he ordered reduction of half salary for tiger guards and feathered forest guards quartered in temples who did not perform duties, and not to give winter clothing;
translations.idiomatic: On gengzi in the eighth month he halved the pay of idle tiger and feathered forest guards in temple quarters and withheld their winter issue;

sentences.zh: 其公卿以下給冬衣之半。

translations.literal: for dukes and ministers downward give half winter clothing.
translations.idiomatic: officials from the Three Dukes down received only half the usual winter clothing allowance.

sentences.zh: 艾縣賊焚燒長沙郡縣，寇益陽，殺令。

translations.literal: Bandits of Ai county burned Changsha commandery and counties, raided Yiyang, and killed the magistrate.
translations.idiomatic: Rebels from Ai county torched Changsha districts, struck Yiyang, and slew the magistrate.

sentences.zh: 又零陵蠻亦叛，寇長沙。

translations.literal: Further the Lingling Man also rebelled and raided Changsha.
translations.idiomatic: The Lingling tribesmen also rose and raided Changsha.

sentences.zh: 己卯，罷琅邪都尉官。

translations.literal: On jimao they abolished the chief commandant office of Langye.
translations.idiomatic: On jimao the chief commandant of Langye was abolished.

sentences.zh: 冬十月，武陵蠻叛，寇江陵，南郡太守李肅坐奔北弃市；

translations.literal: In winter, the tenth month, the Wuling Man rebelled and raided Jiangling; the Administrator of Nan Commandery, Li Su, because of fleeing north was executed in the market;
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth month Wuling tribesmen attacked Jiangling; Li Su, Administrator of Nan, was executed for abandoning his post;

sentences.zh: 辛丑，以太常馮緄為車騎將軍，討之。

translations.literal: on xinchou they appointed Minister of Ceremonies Feng Gun General of Chariots and Cavalry to attack them.
translations.idiomatic: on xinchou Feng Gun, Minister of Ceremonies, was made General of Chariots and Cavalry and sent against them.

sentences.zh: 假公卿以下奉。

translations.literal: They advanced salary from dukes and ministers downward.
translations.idiomatic: Official stipends from the Three Dukes down were paid in advance.

sentences.zh: 又換王侯租以助軍糧，出濯龍中藏錢還之。

translations.literal: They also exchanged princes’ and marquises’ rent to assist military grain, and issued money from the Zhulong central treasury to repay them.
translations.idiomatic: Princes’ and marquises’ rent income was commuted to grain for the army, with reimbursement from the Zhulong treasury.

sentences.zh: 十一月，馮緄大破叛蠻於武陵。

translations.literal: In the eleventh month Feng Gun greatly defeated the rebel Man at Wuling.
translations.idiomatic: In the eleventh month Feng Gun won a major victory over the rebels in Wuling.

sentences.zh: 京兆虎牙都尉宗謙坐臧，下獄死。

translations.literal: Tiger-fang colonel of Jingzhao Zong Qian because of corruption sat in prison and died.
translations.idiomatic: Zong Qian, tiger-fang colonel of Jingzhao, was jailed for corruption and died there.

sentences.zh: 滇那羌寇武威、張掖、酒泉。

translations.literal: The Dianna Qiang raided Wuwei, Zhangye, and Jiuquan.
translations.idiomatic: Dianna Qiang tribes raided Wuwei, Zhangye, and Jiuquan.

sentences.zh: 太尉劉矩免，太常楊秉為太尉。

translations.literal: Grand Commandant Liu Ju was dismissed; Minister of Ceremonies Yang Bing became Grand Commandant.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Ju left the Grand Commandancy; Yang Bing, Minister of Ceremonies, replaced him.

sentences.zh: 夏四月辛亥，康陵東署火。

translations.literal: In summer, the fourth month, on xinhai, the east office of Kangling caught fire.
translations.idiomatic: On xinhai in the fourth month fire broke out at the east office of Kangling.

sentences.zh: 五月，鮮卑寇遼東屬國。

translations.literal: In the fifth month the Xianbei raided the Liaodong dependent state.
translations.idiomatic: In the fifth month Xianbei raiders struck the Liaodong dependent state.

sentences.zh: 秋七月甲申，平陵園寑火。

translations.literal: In autumn, the seventh month, on jiashen, the sleeping hall of Pingling caught fire.
translations.idiomatic: On jiashen in the seventh month the hall at Emperor Zhao’s Pingling burned.

sentences.zh: 桂陽盜賊李研等寇郡界。

translations.literal: Guiyang bandits Li Yan and others raided the commandery borders.
translations.idiomatic: Li Yan and other bandits from Guiyang raided the commandery frontier.

sentences.zh: 武陵蠻復叛，太守陳奉與戰，大破降之。

translations.literal: The Wuling Man again rebelled; Administrator Chen Feng fought them, greatly defeated and made them surrender.
translations.idiomatic: Wuling rose again; Chen Feng, the administrator, defeated them and forced their submission.

sentences.zh: 隴西太守孫羌討滇那羌，破之。

translations.literal: Administrator of Longxi Sun Qiang attacked the Dianna Qiang and defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: Sun Qiang, Administrator of Longxi, crushed the Dianna Qiang.

sentences.zh: 八月，車騎將軍馮緄免。

translations.literal: In the eighth month General of Chariots and Cavalry Feng Gun was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: In the eighth month Feng Gun was removed as General of Chariots and Cavalry.

sentences.zh: 冬十月丙辰，校獵廣成，遂幸函谷關、上林苑。

translations.literal: In winter, the tenth month, on bingchen, he conducted a battue hunt at Guangcheng, then favored Hangu Pass and Shanglin Park.
translations.idiomatic: On bingchen in the tenth month he hunted at Guangcheng, then visited Hangu Pass and Shanglin Park.

sentences.zh: 十一月，司空劉寵免。

translations.literal: In the eleventh month Minister of Works Liu Chong was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: In the eleventh month Liu Chong was removed as Minister of Works.

sentences.zh: 南海賊寇郡界。

translations.literal: Nanhai bandits raided the commandery borders.
translations.idiomatic: Pirates from Nanhai raided the commandery coast.

sentences.zh: 十二月，衛尉周景為司空。

translations.literal: In the twelfth month Commandant of the Guards Zhou Jing became Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: In the twelfth month Zhou Jing, Commandant of the Guards, was appointed Minister of Works.

sentences.zh: 七年春正月庚寅，沛王榮薨。

translations.literal: In the seventh year, spring, the first month, on gengyin, the King of Pei, Rong, died.
translations.idiomatic: On gengyin in the first month of the seventh year of Yanxi Liu Rong, King of Pei, died.

sentences.zh: 三月癸亥，隕石於鄠。

translations.literal: In the third month on guihai a meteorite fell at Hu.
translations.idiomatic: On guihai in the third month a meteorite fell at Hu.

sentences.zh: 夏四月丙寅，梁王成薨。

translations.literal: In summer, the fourth month, on bingyin, the King of Liang, Cheng, died.
translations.idiomatic: On bingyin in the fourth month Liu Cheng, King of Liang, died.

sentences.zh: 五月己丑，京師雨雹。

translations.literal: In the fifth month on jichou hail fell in the capital.
translations.idiomatic: On jichou in the fifth month a hailstorm struck the capital.

sentences.zh: 秋七月辛卯，趙王乾薨。

translations.literal: In autumn, the seventh month, on xinmao, the King of Zhao, Qian, died.
translations.idiomatic: On xinmao in the seventh month Liu Qian, King of Zhao, died.

sentences.zh: 野王山上有死龍。

translations.literal: On Mount Yewang above there was a dead dragon.
translations.idiomatic: A dead dragon was found on Mount Yewang.

sentences.zh: 荊州刺史度尚擊零陵、桂陽盜賊及蠻夷，大破平之。

translations.literal: Inspector of Jing Province Du Shang attacked Lingling and Guiyang bandits and Man and Yi, greatly defeated and pacified them.
translations.idiomatic: Du Shang, inspector of Jing, crushed bandits and tribes in Lingling and Guiyang.

sentences.zh: 冬十月壬寅，南巡狩。

translations.literal: In winter, the tenth month, on renyin, he went south on the imperial hunt.
translations.idiomatic: On renyin in the tenth month he set out on a southern tour.

sentences.zh: 庚申，幸章陵，祠舊宅，遂有事於園廟，賜守令以下各有差。

translations.literal: On gengshen he favored Zhangling, sacrificed at the old residence, then performed rites at the park shrines, bestowing from guardians and magistrates downward each according to gradation.
translations.idiomatic: On gengshen he visited Zhangling, sacrificed at the Liu family’s old home, then offered at the tombs, with gifts graded for every local official down to the keepers.

sentences.zh: 戊辰，幸雲夢，臨漢水；

translations.literal: On wuchen he favored Yunmeng and approached the Han River;
translations.idiomatic: On wuchen he toured Yunmeng and came to the bank of the Han;

sentences.zh: 還，幸新野，祠湖陽、新野公主、魯哀王、壽張敬侯廟。

translations.literal: returning, he favored Xinye and sacrificed at the shrines of the princesses of Huyang and Xinye and Prince Ai of Lu and the Marquis Jing of Shouzhang.
translations.idiomatic: on the return he stopped at Xinye and sacrificed at the shrines of the princesses of Huyang and Xinye, Prince Ai of Lu, and Marquis Jing of Shouzhang.

sentences.zh: 護羌校尉段颎擊當煎羌，破之。

translations.literal: Colonel Protector of the Qiang Duan Jiong attacked the Dangjian Qiang and defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: Duan Jiong defeated the Dangjian Qiang.

sentences.zh: 十二月辛丑，車駕還宮。

translations.literal: In the twelfth month on xinchou the carriage and horses returned to the palace.
translations.idiomatic: On xinchou in the twelfth month the imperial party returned to the capital.

sentences.zh: 八年春正月，遣中常侍左悺之苦縣，祠老子。

translations.literal: In the eighth year, spring, the first month, he sent Palace Attendant Zuo Guan to Ku county to sacrifice to Laozi.
translations.idiomatic: In the first month of the eighth year of Yanxi he sent Zuo Guan, a palace attendant, to Ku county to offer sacrifice to Laozi.

sentences.zh: 勃海王悝謀反，降為廮陶王。

translations.literal: The King of Bohai, Kui, plotted rebellion; he was demoted to King of Yingtao.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Kui, King of Bohai, plotted revolt and was reduced to King of Yingtao.

sentences.zh: 丙申晦，日有食之。

translations.literal: On bingshen the last day of the month, there was a solar eclipse.
translations.idiomatic: At month’s end, bingshen, a solar eclipse occurred.

sentences.zh: 詔公、卿、校尉舉賢良方正。

translations.literal: He ordered dukes, ministers, and colonels to recommend worthy and good, upright in conduct.
translations.idiomatic: He ordered the Three Dukes, ministers, and colonels each to nominate men of integrity.

sentences.zh: 二月己酉，南宮嘉德署黃龍見。

translations.literal: On jiyou in the Southern Palace Jiade office a yellow dragon appeared.
translations.idiomatic: On jiyou a yellow dragon was reported in the Jiade office of the Southern Palace.

sentences.zh: 千秋萬歲殿火。

translations.literal: The Hall of Thousand Autumns and Ten Thousand Years caught fire.
translations.idiomatic: Fire broke out in the Hall of Thousand Autumns and Ten Thousand Years.

sentences.zh: 太僕左稱有辠自殺。

translations.literal: Minister Coachman Zuo Cheng, being guilty, killed himself.
translations.idiomatic: Zuo Cheng, Minister Coachman, took his own life when charged with a crime.

sentences.zh: 癸亥，皇后鄧氏廢。

translations.literal: On guihai Empress of the Deng clan was deposed.
translations.idiomatic: On guihai Empress Deng was deposed.

sentences.zh: 河南尹鄧萬世、虎賁中郎將鄧會下獄死。

translations.literal: Governor of Henan Deng Wanshi and General of the Household for Tiger Guards Deng Hui were cast into prison and died.
translations.idiomatic: Deng Wanshi, Governor of Henan, and Deng Hui, general of the tiger guard, were imprisoned and died there.

sentences.zh: 護羌校尉段颎擊罕姐羌，破之。

translations.literal: Colonel Protector of the Qiang Duan Jiong attacked the Hanjie Qiang and defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: Duan Jiong defeated the Hanjie Qiang.

sentences.zh: 三月辛巳，大赦天下。

translations.literal: In the third month on xinsi there was a great amnesty for all under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: On xinsi in the third month he proclaimed a general amnesty.

sentences.zh: 夏四月甲寅，安陵園寢火。

translations.literal: In summer, the fourth month, on jiayin, the sleeping hall of Anling caught fire.
translations.idiomatic: On jiayin in the fourth month fire damaged the hall at Emperor Hui’s Anling.

sentences.zh: 丁巳，壞郡國諸房祀。

translations.literal: On dingsi they destroyed the various chamber shrines of commanderies and kingdoms.
translations.idiomatic: On dingsi illicit local shrines in commanderies and kingdoms were torn down.

sentences.zh: 濟陰、東郡、濟北河水清。

translations.literal: In Jiyin, Dong commandery, and Jibei north of the river the waters ran clear.
translations.idiomatic: The Yellow River ran clear through Jiyin, Dong commandery, and Jibei.

sentences.zh: 五月壬申，罷太山都尉官。

translations.literal: In the fifth month on renshen they abolished the chief commandant office of Taishan.
translations.idiomatic: On renshen in the fifth month the Taishan chief commandant was abolished.

sentences.zh: 丙戌，太尉楊秉薨。

translations.literal: In the sixth month on bingxu Grand Commandant Yang Bing died.
translations.idiomatic: On bingxu in the sixth month Yang Bing, Grand Commandant, died.

sentences.zh: 六月丙辰，緱氏地裂。

translations.literal: In the sixth month on bingchen the earth split at Goushi.
translations.idiomatic: On bingchen in the sixth month the earth fissured at Goushi.

sentences.zh: 桂陽胡蘭、朱蓋等復反，攻沒郡縣，轉寇零陵，零陵太守陳球拒之；

translations.literal: Hu Lan and Zhu Gai of Guiyang rebelled again, attacked and overwhelmed commanderies and counties, turned to raid Lingling; the Administrator of Lingling, Chen Qiu, resisted them;
translations.idiomatic: Hu Lan and Zhu Gai of Guiyang rose again, overran districts, and swung into Lingling, where Administrator Chen Qiu held the line;

sentences.zh: 遣中郎將度尚、長沙太守抗徐等擊蘭、蓋，大破斬之。

translations.literal: he sent General of the Household Du Shang and Administrator of Changsha Kang Xu and others to attack Lan and Gai, greatly defeated and beheaded them.
translations.idiomatic: Du Shang, General of the Household, and Kang Xu, Administrator of Changsha, were sent and destroyed the rebels, taking their heads.

sentences.zh: 蒼梧太守張敍為賊所執，又桂陽太守任胤背敵畏懦，皆弃市。

translations.literal: Administrator of Cangwu Zhang Xu was seized by bandits; further the Administrator of Guiyang, Ren Yin, turned his back on the enemy in cowardice; both were executed in the market.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Xu, Administrator of Cangwu, was captured by rebels; Ren Yin, Administrator of Guiyang, fled in fear; both were executed in the marketplace.

sentences.zh: 閏月甲午，南宮長秋和歡殿後鉤楯、掖庭、朔平署火。

translations.literal: In the intercalary month on jiawu the Gou dun and lateral courts and Shuoping office behind the Changqiu Hehuan Hall of the Southern Palace caught fire.
translations.idiomatic: On jiawu in the intercalary month fire spread through the hook-rail guard posts and harem offices behind Changqiu’s Hehuan Hall in the Southern Palace.

sentences.zh: 六月，段颎擊當煎羌於湟中，大破之。

translations.literal: In the sixth month Duan Jiong attacked the Dangjian Qiang in the Huangzhong region and greatly defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth month Duan Jiong crushed the Dangjian Qiang in Huangzhong.

sentences.zh: 秋七月，太中大夫陳蕃為太尉。

translations.literal: In autumn, the seventh month, Grandee Counselor Chen Fan became Grand Commandant.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh month Chen Fan, Grandee Counselor, was appointed Grand Commandant.

sentences.zh: 八月戊辰，初令郡國有田者畝斂稅錢。

translations.literal: In the eighth month on wuchen for the first time he ordered that commanderies and kingdoms having fields collect cash tax per mu.
translations.idiomatic: On wuchen in the eighth month he first ordered every commandery and kingdom to collect a cash tax on each mu of cultivated land.

sentences.zh: 九月丁未，京師地震。

translations.literal: In the ninth month on dingwei the capital shook in an earthquake.
translations.idiomatic: On dingwei in the ninth month the capital was shaken by an earthquake.

sentences.zh: 冬十月，司空周景免，太常劉茂為司空。

translations.literal: In winter, the tenth month, Minister of Works Zhou Jing was dismissed; Minister of Ceremonies Liu Mao became Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth month Zhou Jing was removed as Minister of Works; Liu Mao, Minister of Ceremonies, succeeded him.

sentences.zh: 辛巳，立貴人竇氏為皇后。

translations.literal: On xinsi he established Honored Lady Dou as empress.
translations.idiomatic: On xinsi Lady Dou was made empress.

sentences.zh: 勃海妖賊蓋登等稱『太上皇帝』，有玉印、珪、璧、鐵券，相署置，皆伏誅。

translations.literal: Sorcerer-bandits of Bohai, Gai Deng and others, called themselves Supreme Emperor, had jade seals, scepters, disks, and iron tallies, and set up offices for one another; all were executed.
translations.idiomatic: Gai Deng and other cult leaders in Bohai styled themselves Supreme Emperor with forged regalia; all were put to death.

sentences.zh: 十一月壬子，德陽殿西閤、黃門北寺火，延及廣義、神虎門，燒殺人。

translations.literal: In the eleventh month on renzi the west pavilion of Deyang Hall and the Northern Office of the Yellow Gates caught fire; it spread to Guangyi and Shenhu gates and burned people to death.
translations.idiomatic: On renzi in the eleventh month fire broke out in Deyang’s west wing and the eunuchs’ Northern Prison, spread to Guangyi and Shenhu gates, and killed people in the flames.

sentences.zh: 使中常侍管霸之苦縣，祠老子。

translations.literal: He sent Palace Attendant Guan Ba to Ku county to sacrifice to Laozi.
translations.idiomatic: He sent Guan Ba, a palace attendant, to Ku county to sacrifice to Laozi.

sentences.zh: 九年春正月辛卯朔，日有食之。

translations.literal: In the ninth year, spring, the first month, on xinmao the first day of the month, there was a solar eclipse.
translations.idiomatic: On new year’s day of the ninth year of Yanxi, xinmao, a solar eclipse occurred.

sentences.zh: 詔公、卿、校尉、郡國舉至孝。

translations.literal: He ordered dukes, ministers, colonels, and commanderies and kingdoms to recommend men of utmost filial piety.
translations.idiomatic: He ordered the Three Dukes, ministers, colonels, and every commandery and kingdom each to recommend a paragon of filial devotion.

sentences.zh: 沛國戴異得黃金印，無文字，遂與廣陵人龍尚等共祭井，作符書，稱『太上皇』，伏誅。

translations.literal: Dai Yi of Pei obtained a gold seal without writing, then with Long Shang of Guangling and others jointly sacrificed at a well, made talisman writings, and called himself Supreme Emperor; they were executed.
translations.idiomatic: Dai Yi of Pei found an unmarked gold seal, then with Long Shang of Guangling held well-side rites and forged talismans proclaiming a Supreme Emperor; all were executed.

sentences.zh: 己酉，詔曰：『比歲不登，民多飢窮，又有水旱疾疫之困。

translations.literal: On jiyou an edict said: "In recent years the harvests have not ripened; the people are much hungry and destitute; moreover there are the troubles of flood, drought, sickness, and pestilence."
translations.idiomatic: On jiyou an edict ran: "Years of poor harvests have left the people destitute, compounded by flood, drought, and disease."

sentences.zh: 盜賊徵發，南州尤甚。

translations.literal: Banditry and levies—the southern provinces have been especially severe.
translations.idiomatic: Rebellion and conscription have hit the south worst of all.

sentences.zh: 灾異日食，譴告累至。

translations.literal: Disasters and prodigies and solar eclipses—reproaches and warnings have come in succession.
translations.idiomatic: Portents and eclipses have followed one another as Heaven’s rebukes.

sentences.zh: 政亂在予，仍獲咎徵。

translations.literal: Government is in disorder in me; still I receive blame-omens.
translations.idiomatic: The fault lies with my rule, yet I bear the signs of reproach.

sentences.zh: 其令大司農絕今歲調度徵求，及前年所調未畢者，勿復收責。

translations.literal: Let it be ordered that the Minister of Finance cut off this year’s transfers and exactions, and those of the year before not yet completed, and do not again collect or demand them.
translations.idiomatic: The Minister of Finance shall cancel this year’s levies and any unpaid exactions from the year before and cease dunning for them.

sentences.zh: 其灾旱盜賊之郡，勿收租，餘郡悉半入。』

translations.literal: In commanderies and kingdoms suffering disaster, drought, or banditry, do not collect rent; the remaining commanderies all half pay in.
translations.idiomatic: Commanderies ravaged by disaster, drought, or rebellion are exempt from land tax; everywhere else only half the usual tax is due.

sentences.zh: 三月癸巳，京師有火光轉行，人相驚譟。

translations.literal: In the third month on guisi in the capital there was fire light moving in transit; people frightened one another with clamor.
translations.idiomatic: On guisi in the third month strange lights moved across the capital sky and set the crowds in panic.

sentences.zh: 司隸、豫州飢死者什四五，至有滅戶者，遣三府掾賑稟之。

translations.literal: In the Metropolitan Province and Yuzhou forty or fifty percent of those who died of famine reached even to households extinguished; he dispatched clerks of the three offices to relieve and issue grain.
translations.idiomatic: In the capital region and Yuzhou four or five in ten starved to death, some whole families perishing; staff from the three chief offices were sent with relief grain.

sentences.zh: 陳留太守韋毅坐臧自殺。

translations.literal: Administrator of Chenliu Wei Yi because of corruption killed himself.
translations.idiomatic: Wei Yi, Administrator of Chenliu, committed suicide when charged with corruption.

sentences.zh: 夏四月，濟陰、東郡、濟北、平原河水清。

translations.literal: In summer, the fourth month, in Jiyin, Dong commandery, Jibei, and Pingyuan north of the river the waters ran clear.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth month the Yellow River ran clear through Jiyin, Dong commandery, Jibei, and Pingyuan.

sentences.zh: 司徒許栩免。

translations.literal: Minister of Education Xu Xu was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: Xu Xu was removed as Minister of Education.

sentences.zh: 五月，太常胡廣為司徒。

translations.literal: In the fifth month Minister of Ceremonies Hu Guang became Minister of Education.
translations.idiomatic: In the fifth month Hu Guang, Minister of Ceremonies, was appointed Minister of Education.

sentences.zh: 六月，南匈奴及烏桓、鮮卑寇緣邊九郡。

translations.literal: In the sixth month the Southern Xiongnu together with Wuhuan and Xianbei raided nine commanderies along the frontier.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth month Southern Xiongnu, Wuhuan, and Xianbei jointly raided nine border commanderies.

sentences.zh: 秋七月，沈氐羌寇武威、張掖。

translations.literal: In autumn, the seventh month, the Shenshi Qiang raided Wuwei and Zhangye.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh month Shenshi Qiang horsemen struck Wuwei and Zhangye.

sentences.zh: 詔舉武猛，三公各二人，卿、校尉各一人。

translations.literal: He ordered recommendation of men bold and fierce in war: the Three Dukes each two, ministers and colonels each one.
translations.idiomatic: He called for nominations of martially outstanding men—two from each of the Three Dukes and one from each minister and colonel.

sentences.zh: 太尉陳蕃免。

translations.literal: Grand Commandant Chen Fan was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Fan was removed as Grand Commandant.

sentences.zh: 庚午，祠黃、老於濯龍宮。

translations.literal: On gengwu he sacrificed to the Yellow Emperor and Laozi at Zhulong Palace.
translations.idiomatic: On gengwu he offered sacrifice to the Yellow Emperor and Laozi in Zhulong Palace.

sentences.zh: 遣使匈奴中郎將張奐擊南匈奴、烏桓、鮮卑。

translations.literal: He sent General of the Household for Dependent States Zhang Huan to attack the Southern Xiongnu, Wuhuan, and Xianbei.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Huan, General of the Household for Dependent States, was dispatched against the Southern Xiongnu, Wuhuan, and Xianbei.

sentences.zh: 九月，光祿勳周景為太尉。

translations.literal: In the ninth month Household Minister Zhou Jing became Grand Commandant.
translations.idiomatic: In the ninth month Zhou Jing, Household Minister, was appointed Grand Commandant.

sentences.zh: 南陽太守成瑨、太原太守劉質，並以譖弃市。

translations.literal: Administrator of Nanyang Cheng Jin and Administrator of Taiyuan Liu Zhi, both because of slander were executed in the market.
translations.idiomatic: Cheng Jin of Nanyang and Liu Zhi of Taiyuan were both slandered into execution in the marketplace.

sentences.zh: 司空劉茂免。

translations.literal: Minister of Works Liu Mao was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Mao was removed as Minister of Works.

sentences.zh: 大秦國王遣使奉獻。

translations.literal: The king of Daqin sent envoys to present tribute.
translations.idiomatic: The ruler of the Roman East sent envoys with tribute.

sentences.zh: 冬十二月，洛城傍竹柏枯傷。

translations.literal: In winter, the twelfth month, bamboo and cypress beside the Luo walls withered and were injured.
translations.idiomatic: In the twelfth month bamboo and cypress along the Luoyang wall withered as if blighted.

sentences.zh: 光祿勳汝南宣酆為司空。

translations.literal: Household Minister Xuan Feng of Runan became Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: Xuan Feng of Runan, Household Minister, became Minister of Works.

sentences.zh: 南匈奴、烏桓率眾詣張奐降。

translations.literal: The Southern Xiongnu and Wuhuan led their masses to Zhang Huan and surrendered.
translations.idiomatic: The Southern Shanyu and Wuhuan brought their followers to Zhang Huan and submitted.

sentences.zh: 司隸校尉李膺等二百餘人受誣為黨人，並坐下獄，書名王府。

translations.literal: Metropolitan Governor Li Ying and more than two hundred others were falsely accused as faction members; all sat in prison together, their names written at the royal mansion.
translations.idiomatic: Li Ying, Metropolitan Governor, and over two hundred others were framed as faction partisans, imprisoned, and their names registered for proscription.

sentences.zh: 永康元年春正月，先零羌寇三輔，中郎將張奐破平之。

translations.literal: In the first year of Yongkang, spring, the first month, the Xianling Qiang raided the three adjunct capitals; General of the Household Zhang Huan defeated and pacified them.
translations.idiomatic: In the first month of Yongkang, 167, Xianling Qiang raided the capital region; Zhang Huan, General of the Household, crushed and pacified them.

sentences.zh: 當煎羌寇武威，護羌校尉段颎追擊於鸞鳥，大破之。

translations.literal: The Dangjian Qiang raided Wuwei; Colonel Protector of the Qiang Duan Jiong pursued and attacked them at Luanniao and greatly defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: Dangjian Qiang struck Wuwei; Duan Jiong overtook them at Luanniao and won a major victory.

sentences.zh: 西羌悉平。

translations.literal: The Western Qiang were all pacified.
translations.idiomatic: The western Qiang were entirely subdued.

sentences.zh: 夫餘王寇玄菟，太守公孫域與戰，破之。

translations.literal: The king of Puyu raided Xuantu; Administrator Gongsun Yu fought him and defeated him.
translations.idiomatic: The king of Puyu attacked Xuantu; Gongsun Yu, the administrator, met him in battle and won.

sentences.zh: 夏四月，先零羌寇三輔。

translations.literal: In summer, the fourth month, the Xianling Qiang raided the three adjunct capitals.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth month Xianling Qiang again raided the capital region.

sentences.zh: 五月丙申，京師及上黨地裂。

translations.literal: In the fifth month on bingshen the capital and Shangdang shook in an earth fissure.
translations.idiomatic: On bingshen in the fifth month the earth split in the capital and in Shangdang.

sentences.zh: 廬江賊起，寇郡界。

translations.literal: Bandits of Lujiang rose and raided the commandery borders.
translations.idiomatic: Rebels rose in Lujiang and plundered the commandery.

sentences.zh: 壬子晦，日有食之。

translations.literal: On renzi the last day of the month, there was a solar eclipse.
translations.idiomatic: At month’s end, renzi, a solar eclipse occurred.

sentences.zh: 詔公、卿、校尉舉賢良方正。

translations.literal: He ordered dukes, ministers, and colonels to recommend worthy and good, upright in conduct.
translations.idiomatic: He ordered the Three Dukes, ministers, and colonels to nominate candidates of integrity.

sentences.zh: 六月庚申，大赦天下，悉除黨錮，改元永康。

translations.literal: In the sixth month on gengshen there was a great amnesty for all under Heaven; all partisan prohibitions were removed; the era name was changed to Yongkang.
translations.idiomatic: On gengshen in the sixth month he proclaimed a general amnesty, lifted the ban on the faction lists, and adopted the era name Yongkang.

sentences.zh: 丙寅，阜陵王統薨。

translations.literal: On bingyin the King of Fuling, Tong, died.
translations.idiomatic: On bingyin Liu Tong, King of Fuling, died.

sentences.zh: 秋八月，魏郡言嘉禾生，甘露降。

translations.literal: In autumn, the eighth month, Weijun reported auspicious grain growing and sweet dew descending.
translations.idiomatic: In the eighth month Weijun reported twin-eared grain and falling sweet dew.

sentences.zh: 巴郡言黃龍見。

translations.literal: Bajun reported a yellow dragon seen.
translations.idiomatic: Ba commandery reported a yellow dragon sighting.

sentences.zh: 六州大水，勃海海溢。

translations.literal: Six provinces had great flooding; the Bohai sea overflowed.
translations.idiomatic: Six provinces were inundated and the Bohai coast flooded.

sentences.zh: 詔州郡賜溺死者七歲以上錢，人二千；

translations.literal: He ordered that commanderies bestow on those drowned dead seven sui and upward cash, each person two thousand;
translations.idiomatic: He ordered local authorities to grant two thousand cash for each flood victim seven sui or older;

sentences.zh: 一家皆被害者，悉為收斂；

translations.literal: where whole families were all harmed, all were collected and buried for them;
translations.idiomatic: where entire households had perished, the state would gather and bury the dead;

sentences.zh: 其亡失榖食，稟人三斛。

translations.literal: as for those who lost grain and food, issue grain three hu per person.
translations.idiomatic: survivors who had lost their grain stores were given three hu each.

sentences.zh: 冬十月，先零羌寇三輔，使匈奴中郎將張奐擊破之。

translations.literal: In winter, the tenth month, the Xianling Qiang raided the three adjunct capitals; he sent General of the Household for Dependent States Zhang Huan to attack and defeat them.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth month Xianling Qiang raided the capital region again; Zhang Huan, General of the Household for Dependent States, defeated them.

sentences.zh: 十一月，西河言白菟見。

translations.literal: In the eleventh month Xihe reported a white hare seen.
translations.idiomatic: In the eleventh month Xihe reported a white hare.

sentences.zh: 十二月壬申，復廮陶王悝為勃海王。

translations.literal: In the twelfth month on renshen he restored the King of Yingtao, Kui, as King of Bohai.
translations.idiomatic: On renshen in the twelfth month Liu Kui, lately King of Yingtao, was restored as King of Bohai.

sentences.zh: 丁丑，帝崩於德陽前殿。

translations.literal: On dingchou the emperor died in the front hall of Deyang.
translations.idiomatic: On dingchou he died in the front hall of Deyang Palace.

sentences.zh: 年三十六。

translations.literal: His years were thirty-six.
translations.idiomatic: He was thirty-six sui old.

sentences.zh: 戊寅，尊皇后曰皇太后，太后臨朝。

translations.literal: On wuyin they honored the empress as empress dowager; the empress dowager presided over court from behind the screen.
translations.idiomatic: On wuyin the empress was elevated to empress dowager and began to rule as regent.

sentences.zh: 是歲，復博陵、河間二郡，比豐、沛。

translations.literal: That year they restored Boling and Hejian as two commanderies, comparable to Feng and Pei.
translations.idiomatic: That year Boling and Hejian were again made full commanderies, with privileges like Feng and Pei.

sentences.zh: 論曰：前史稱桓帝好音樂，善琴笙。

translations.literal: The discussion says: The earlier histories state that Emperor Huan loved music and was skilled at zither and mouth-organ.
translations.idiomatic: The historian’s judgment: Earlier annals say Emperor Huan loved music and played the zither and sheng well.

sentences.zh: 飾芳林而考濯龍之宮，設華蓋以祠浮圖、老子，斯將所謂『聽於神』乎！

translations.literal: He adorned Fanglin and inspected the palace at Zhulong; he set up a flowered canopy to sacrifice to the Buddha and Laozi—is this not what is called listening to the spirits!
translations.idiomatic: He ornamented Fanglin Grove and sacrificed at Zhulong to the Buddha and Laozi beneath a ritual canopy—rule swayed by cult and portent rather than by sober policy!

sentences.zh: 及誅梁冀，奮威怒，天下猶企其休息。

translations.literal: When Liang Ji was executed and he roused his might and wrath, all under Heaven still hoped he would rest and recover.
translations.idiomatic: When Liang Ji fell and the emperor shook off that tyranny, the realm still hoped for peace and recovery.

sentences.zh: 而五邪嗣虐，流衍四方。

translations.literal: Yet the five evils succeeded in cruelty and flowed and spread to the four quarters.
translations.idiomatic: Instead the five palace favorites carried on the cruelty, and their poison spread across the empire.

sentences.zh: 自非忠賢力爭，屢折姦鋒，雖願依斟流彘，亦不可得已。

translations.literal: If it had not been for the loyal and worthy striving in force, repeatedly breaking the edge of wickedness, even the wish to cling to Zhenxun or drift to Zhi could not have been realized.
translations.idiomatic: Without loyal ministers who again and again broke the traitors’ momentum, the Han could not have survived even the sort of calamity that in older days drove kings to the refuges of Zhenxun or Zhi.

sentences.zh: 贊曰：桓自宗支，越躋天祿。

translations.literal: The encomium says: Huan came from a collateral branch and leapt up to Heaven’s blessing.
translations.idiomatic: Encomium: Raised from a cadet line, Huan ascended to the throne.

sentences.zh: 政移五幸，刑淫三獄。

translations.literal: Government shifted to the five favorites; punishments ran riot in the three prisons.
translations.idiomatic: Power passed to the five favorites; justice drowned in the three prison bureaus.

sentences.zh: 傾宮雖積，皇身靡續。

translations.literal: Though leaning palaces piled up in excess, the imperial person had no successor.
translations.idiomatic: Palaces rose in wasteful splendor, yet he left no heir.
